30000.event: Father took him to forbidden dragon tomb; he saw glowing bones. His eyes turned gold.

30001.event: Father executed by Dragon Breath Cult for smuggling. He inherited map and buyer list, first transaction at Ash Bazaar.

30002.event: At a dragon bone auction, a masked servant gave him a dragon egg and a note to flee.

30003.event: Egg hatched silver wyrmling. He felt pain of being recognized. Dragon mark on arm; his body dragonized.

30004.event: Bounty placed on him. Fled with wyrmling to Abyssal Rift; opened a living dragon blood shop in underground market.

30005.event: Wounded red dragon crashed; he promised to return dragon soul crystal to sanctuary for its dying fire.

30006.event: Dragon Soul Guardian pierced his chest; mark shattered. He collapsed, saved by mysterious force, lost life but survived.

30007.event: He became a legendary dragon blood smuggler but went blind from residual dragon breath, seeing only soul flames.

30008.event: The Dragon Breath Cult's High Priest bought dragon blood to extend life, but it poisoned him for his impure faith.

30009.event: He went blind that night and dreamed a petrified hatchling dragon leading him to a forgotten tomb of star prophecies.

30010.event: He awakened a star dragon by pouring dragon blood, and it reshaped his eyes to see fate threads.

30011.event: The Dragon Vein Seer guided adventurers from calamity, died saving a city in an earthquake, and became a dragon mountain.

30012.event: Moon lily in foster father's garden led him to Dreamlands, where a dream baku mistook him for kin and gave him a dream-cutting glass blade.

30013.event: Alarmed by his daytime translucency, his foster father sent him to a temple to defeat nightmare maggot in a dream.

30014.event: A noble lady had him retrieve a will from a dream tomb, evading a mimic by sharing a childhood memory.

30015.event: A Gray Rain Plague caused eternal sleep. He rallied dream travelers to stop a soul-stealing sorcerer building a divine realm.

30016.event: He cut the sorcerer's realm wall with a glass knife, losing his left arm to its rules; no healing worked.

30017.event: After the war, he became a Dream Keeper running Forget Me Not, helping retrieve names, pets, lost love from dreams.

30018.event: A knight's dream request to retrieve a horse's soul reveals his desertion caused comrades' deaths, the horse their resentment.

30019.event: He restored a lost civilization in dreams, but its architecture corrupted reality, causing dizziness and memory loss.

30020.event: To sever the entanglement between dreams and reality, he destroyed the Floating City with a glass knife.

30021.event: Seven years later, a little girl he had once helped found his last fragment in a dream and woke him.

30022.event: In his later years, he wrote 'Dream Wanderings,' which became a textbook for dream mages of later generations.

30023.event: At birth, a dark red curse mark appeared on his forehead.

30024.event: The monastery mentors discovered he could absorb weak curses from others without dying and began training him as a 'curse.

30025.event: He lost his left eye breaking a curse on a girl, who then became his first follower.

30026.event: A year before the clan's curse erupted, he went alone to the ancestral tomb and discovered the curse's source was.

30027.event: He absorbed curses; his rune-covered skin eased curse pain. The poor saw him as a relic.

30028.event: He channeled a plague curse into himself, but it shattered his life. He became rune fragments, sealing the scream.

30029.event: He used dragon lich meditations to turn curses into blessings, saving a city but losing his ability to walk.

30030.event: On a lunar eclipse night, a dragon lich sealed his rebel soul in his spine, lost hearing, and died smiling.

30031.event: He sealed a remnant soul, heard curse frequencies from bone vibration, and removed curses by tapping his staff.

30032.event: He solved the family curse by performing a ritual that turned him into a stone statue smiling, pointing skyward with his staff.

30033.event: Touching the astrolabe foretold her death at thirty during a meteor shower; her mentor hid it.

30034.event: She completed a star chart, found a dark star. She coughed star dust, decayed, and turned into star dust.

30035.event: Mapping ley lines, she met a star spirit, glimpsed ancient gods, and her soul was taken into the star sea.

30036.event: Eclipse consumed her completing the Star Map, and she died with white hair scattered, leaving the map her sole legacy.

30037.event: Hunted as a star-blasphemer, she fled with a star-speaker thief, learning to hide in starlight.

30038.event: She completed star chart, saw dark star would hit main star on her 30th birthday, decided to alter orbit.

30039.event: She burned her life to draw a star chart, deflected a dark star, became dust seeding stars, rewriting the prophecy.

30040.event: His mother withered from an unfair elf pact, so he vowed to study contract loopholes across races.

30041.event: You entered Eldland's rune law school, studied supernatural contract law, and in mock trial made a devil professor compensate a summoner for soul wear.

30042.event: He became a rune lawyer, sued a tree elf for root damage, found a clause and won.

30043.event: A dying dragonborn hired him to draft a will; he found a clause challenging contract abuse.

30044.event: He sued a dragon for enslaving a village using a clause, summoned the Oath Dragon, won contract void and taxes.

30045.event: As chief lawyer, he drafted the Non-Human Rights Bill, sparking backlash and surviving assassination attempts via contract traps.

30046.event: A high-ranking devil used his mother's soul to demand bill loopholes, then reclaimed her soul and banished himself.

30047.event: Bill passed, races coexisted. Hailed as 'Chain of Contracts', he wrote a Rune Code commentary for ten years.

30048.event: He died peacefully after finishing the Codex; foes sent golden pages; his epitaph declared all rights achievable.

30049.event: Born in a dilapidated castle cellar, the umbilical cord entwined with the family's only remaining silver shield emblem.

30050.event: He watched bandits burn his ancestral home, then found a shattered family motto plate among the ashes, knuckles white.

30051.event: He joined the Griffin Knights as a squire and won a tournament with a wooden sword, defeating three retainers.

30052.event: While garrisoning the Thorn Border, he saw a dragon-slaying hero fall; scales embedded in his armor and nightmares.

30053.event: Given the Riverlands, he sensed conspiracy at a banquet and studied statecraft and ancient law.

30054.event: The ancient dragon Scorched Wing awoke, melting the fief. He charged, lost his arm, and died clutching his sword.

30055.event: He drew the holy sword Morning Silence in a king's tomb, its glow silvering his hair and repelling dragons.

30056.event: Fearing his reputation, the king stripped his title for entering the forbidden tomb and exiled him north.

30057.event: He traded half his life for dragon blood, then died smiling in a blizzard.

30058.event: Alone, he flew over charred mountains and thrust the holy sword into Scorched Wing; both fell into lava.

30059.event: The dean sighed and picked up the crying abandoned infant wrapped in an old robe left at the academy's threshold.

30060.event: He found a half-charred page of Necromantic Truths in the forbidden section, copying it by moonlight, eyes turning purple.

30061.event: Branded for drawing a lich's ritual circle, he was stripped of his badge and exiled into the storm.

30062.event: He bet half his soul for a phylactery fragment, but a contract put him to sleep; body became tearstones.

30063.event: In Silent Valley, his first lich transformation stopped his heart, froze his young face, and turned left finger bones translucent.

30064.event: Holy Flame Knights' Soul Purge Crusade saw him fill a valley with dead to repel siege, earning title Bone Count.

30065.event: On the Dead Plain, he built an obsidian tower and was made Seeker of forbidden tomes by the Lich King.

30066.event: He probed creation's secrets, incurring the gods' Void Punishment that stripped his emotions and memories.

30067.event: The forcibly fused demigod sparked in mortal flesh, erased with tower and ambition, leaving a cold flame like a sigh.

30068.event: The obsidian tower crumbled silently; the undying body smiled, closed its eyes, its soul scattered as dim stardust.

30069.event: Born in Eversong's sacred nest, Elune's silver radiance filled her like mother's milk.

30070.event: I first drew mother's white rattan longbow, and arrows tracked wind's breath and shot a dead branch 300 steps away.

30071.event: At Moonlight Pool, unicorns chose her as Forest Speaker, letting her understand every leaf's whisper.

30072.event: She shot seven axe handles, built a briar wall, and drove hundreds from the woodland alone.

30073.event: The empire's forest-burning army swarmed like locusts; the sacred tree Eversong wailed in dragon fire for three days before turning.

30074.event: Deep in the corrupted Shadow Forest, she traded half her heart to awaken the last treant, creating a new oasis.

30075.event: As the enemy army advanced once more, she lay peacefully among the roots of the newborn sacred tree; her soul.

30076.event: The midwife screamed and threw the golden-eyed newborn with faint scales onto a haystack.

30077.event: His first dragon transformation blackened his arm and burned the barn, so he fled into the mountains.

30078.event: Found by a retired dragonslayer, he meditated under a waterfall to suppress dragon blood and retract scales.

30079.event: Humiliated at the market, he unleashed crimson wings, destroying half a street and exposing his identity.

30080.event: Alone entering the ancestors' Dragonbone Graveyard, enduring a thousand years of scorching memories in the Dragon Soul Trial, his eye.

30081.event: The dragon frenzy completely out of control for the first time, turning into a shadow of destruction over the capital.

30082.event: Ice cracks, he breaks free, heeds his bloodline's call, spreads wings, and flies to the Dragon Kingdom.

30083.event: On the dome of the Dragon Kingdom, he perishes together with the ancient dragon progenitor, his body exploding into countless.

30084.event: Born in the Holy Light Cathedral's inner sanctum, golden prayers on his skin, he was proclaimed the Chosen Saint.

30085.event: At the coming-of-age ceremony, he publicly questions 'Why do the gods allow the innocent to suffer?

30086.event: Barbarians breach the Holy City, burn the temple; he lies bloodied, watching impaled bishops on flagpoles, expressionless.

30087.event: He tries countless suicide methods, endures agony each time, then studies the curse's structure.

30088.event: Becomes the Gray-robed Sage at the Desert of Truth's edge, secretly guiding heroes against shadows, never traveling with them.

30089.event: Sitting on a mountaintop watching yet another human royal city being swallowed by the demonic tide, he strokes his petrified.

30090.event: He, entered, godfall, pierced, his, heart, with, a, hidden, blood, dagger, and, dissolved, into, light, for, a, future, without, him.

30091.event: You are born in a small village, with scale-like birthmarks on your skin; the midwife screams in fright.

30092.event: The villagers reject you as a 'monster'; your mother tearfully abandons you at the edge of the forest.

30093.event: An injured ancient dragon discovers you, whispers draconic secrets, and the dragon blood inside you boils for the first time.

30094.event: You wander to a magic academy, taken in by an old mage, learning to control dragonfire.

30095.event: Dark mages attack the academy; you first transform into a half-dragon, repelling them but scaring your friends.

30096.event: You leave the academy, embarking on a journey to find the truth about dragons, joining the Adventurers' Guild.

30097.event: In the Dragon's Lair Abyss, you find your mother's regretful letter, learning you are the ancient Dragon Vein Guardian's descendant.

30098.event: You inherit the duty of the Dragon Vein Guardian, defeat an evil red dragon, and are recognized by the dragons.

30099.event: You transform into an ancient dragon form, clad in emerald scales, guarding the northern mountains of Eldraine.

30100.event: Dragon Vein exhausted, you fell in a battle against the Abyss Legion, your dragon soul returned to heaven.

30101.event: Born in Deepforge City's lava-lit blacksmith family, ancient rune-carved walls foretold your fate.

30102.event: Selected by a Rune Master, you entered the Rune Hall to study, showing astonishing memory.

30103.event: Unintentionally discovered a Titan rune stone slab beneath the hall, you secretly copied it and learned 'Stone Speech'.

30104.event: Deepforge City was attacked by underground monsters. You activated the ancient defensive colossus with runes, saving the city.

30105.event: You journeyed with elven scholars to find the lost Stellar Forging Method and solve an ancient mystery.

30106.event: A failed rune experiment opened a subspace rift; the Entropy Demon's backlash cursed you, dissipating your soul with the Dragon.

30107.event: The continent fell into shadow. Bearing guilt, you sought a way to reseal the Entropy Demon together with adventurers.

30108.event: You sealed the Entropy Demon into your obsidian arm, ending your legend like a candle in the wind.

30109.event: You retired to an underground chamber in Deepforge City, guarding a terrible secret until you were forgotten.

30110.event: Your rune right arm shattered, freeing the Entropy Demon; you detonated the bomb, perishing together.

30111.event: In Moonlight Grove, the World Tree wove a cradle for you, and the Elven Queen named you Star Song.

30112.event: At the Elven Court's spring festival, your song made flowers bloom out of season, crowning you Weaver of Songs.

30113.event: Weary of eternal beauty, you posed as a poet, your songs stirring the city-states.

30114.event: You fell in love with a valiant human paladin, using elven secret arts to intertwine your fates.

30115.event: Your beloved died in the Battle of Ashen Plains; your grief summoned the Angel of Death.

30116.event: You challenged the Angel of Death, restoring your beloved's soul with your song, but at the cost of your immortality.

30118.event: Your beloved passed away peacefully. You sang your last song before their grave and turned into a singing stone statue.

30119.event: Stars shifted, magic tides swept Eldland, awakening mortals and attracting ancient beings from planar rifts.

30120.event: The ancient dragon Elashus awakens, its breath melting three cities, calling for dragon slayers.

30121.event: Black Death Fog from the east turns infected into stiff corpses that attack the living, dimming holy light.

30122.event: The city-state of Kazaris declares a monopoly on mithril veins, triggering a continental trade war.

30123.event: Jade Tower's three mage schools' war over taboo research tore its dome and rained lost tomes, mixing knowledge with ruin.

30124.event: The Golden Register unseals for the centennial tournament; knights and rangers risk all for the sword of glory.

30125.event: Demon prince tears a rift of eternal night. Legions surge like black tide. Eldran Alliance forms desperate lines.

30126.event: By courage and faith, you passed Goddess Oriana's twelve trials at Silverpeak; divine grace remade your body and healed wounds.

30127.event: Archmage reveals stone-to-gold alchemy, collapsing economy, sparking famine and riots.

30128.event: Elemental planes collide, tearing sky with fire rain, hail, iron gales. Lords lead monstrous hordes across borders.

30129.event: The last twilight descends, stars fall, divine storms rage, prayers fail—only the sword decides fate.

30130.event: The Council of the Dead summons vengeful heroes who obey bloodlines and burn their kin.

30131.event: A magic sea surges, awakening bloodlines and granting peasants power, but storms tear reality apart.

30132.event: Abyss opens in Dead Wastelands. Bone legions march south. You answer the call and die, joining the wailing host.

30133.event: Dragon wings darken sky, volcanoes spew lava and shadows, ancient dragons return furious, scales become gold coins luring slayers to doom.

30134.event: A mad mage reveals time secrets. The Ninth School is born, enabling eternal youth but tearing spacetime cracks.

30135.event: A crimson Philosopher's Stone crashes gold's value, leaving mints scrap and corpses starving.

30136.event: Emerald Court horns sound. Arrow rain and knight charge clash. You charge into magical fire and are consumed.

30137.event: A blacksmith pulls sword from stone, knighted on dragon, inspires youths to join Adventurers' Guild.

30138.event: An ancient pulse drives dungeon monsters berserk, flooding labyrinth exits; you guard the wall until overwhelmed.

30139.event: As the Crimson Moon peaks, madness drives you to join the slaughter.

30140.event: The tsunami receded, a rune-covered tower rose with a divine shard. Popes and kings sent explorers to compete for godhood.

30141.event: A curse crystallizes skin into glass sculptures. You shatter into colored light, your elegy joins the chorus.

30142.event: The Silver Wings guild split over dragon bloodline views; former comrades became foes, prices dropped, missions trap-filled.

30143.event: In a tavern, you lost a silver coin on a dragon bet, gaining a scribbled useless organs guide.
30143.postEvent: You found three errors in a guide. The tavern keeper wants to buy it for decoration.

30144.event: A gnome sold you a fake invisibility cloak; wearing that tattered cloth made you stand out.
30144.postEvent: The innkeeper called you Transparent Knight and got you a wedding job as an invisible person.

30145.event: Your shadow becomes a dragon shape. A mage says it's a playful spell lasting one day and refuses to dispel.

30146.event: After freeing a mage from a tree, his Lucky Potion gave you rainbow hiccups all night—you had fun.

30147.event: You attend a masquerade where guests are skeletons. You flee. Next day, you learn it was the necromancer's annual social.
30147.postEvent: The guild receptionist wrote in your evaluation: socializing with the undead needs improvement.

30148.event: You search three days for a dwarf's missing stone spirit, only to find it asleep in his pocket.

30149.event: A statue's eyes follow you. You speak to it, and it sneezes, burying you in thousand-year dust.
30149.postEvent: You then noticed your lung capacity increased, letting you swim the whole moat on one breath.

30150.event: Bard sang you as clown on lame donkey chased by goose; for month, people brayed.

30151.event: You used a milk-making ring to earn money, but the Dairy Guild fined and confiscated it for lacking a license.

30152.event: A star cartographer prophesied you'd tread on dragon dung; you scoffed, yet did, and a gardener bought the warm heap.

30153.event: You woke with blue hair from Blue Moon Sickness, gaining favor with noblewomen at a ball.

30154.event: Letter: 'Don't eat tomorrow's mushroom soup.' You ate; nothing. Emblem: future you sent it.
30154.postEvent: To this day, you still can't figure out why your future self would send that letter.

30155.event: Crow 'Temporary Master' repeated secrets, cost you friends. On day 8, left a black feather.

30156.event: While chasing an enemy, you fell into a time rift, missing knighthood but escaping a plague.
30156.postEvent: Looking at the empty streets, a chill ran down your spine.

30157.event: You kissed a talking toad claiming to be a knight; it hiccuped rainbow smoke and hopped away, nothing changed.
30157.postEvent: Afterwards, you noticed a faint lavender scent on your palm that lasted for a week.

30158.event: A merchant gave you Dragon's Breath Candy; your hiccups now produce flames that burn your homework.
30158.postEvent: Your teacher refused to believe that excuse and made you copy the 'Fireball Safety Code' ten times.

30159.event: You found a wordless diary in a treehouse; drawing dinner created a wax-and-ink tasting copy.
30159.postEvent: You tried for a week until it suddenly failed, then drew a cake only to get a camphor-scented sponge.

30160.event: While playing hide-and-seek, you hid in armor that moved and crashed into the mayor's carriage.
30160.postEvent: You were banned from metal statues; the mayor still blames a wandering knight's prank.

30161.event: Your reflection stops imitating you, winks, and shushes. Since then, your mirror smile is half a second slower.
30161.postEvent: It caused no real harm, except that every time you look in the mirror, it gives you the creeps.

30162.event: Finding a star-map pebble, you dreamed of a transparent dragon on the Milky Way; it foretold rain, which came.
30162.postEvent: Your weather predictions always came true for the next day, and the pebble slowly shrank.

30163.event: Sealed scroll gave shadow life; it stole snacks, took dangerous test for you, earning full marks.
30163.postEvent: The academy noticed and confiscated the scroll, but your shadow still sneaks out to play while you sleep.

30164.event: You scratch a talking tree's itchy bark, dropping acorns on a goblin. He gives you a magic marble.
30164.postEvent: The tree's silver leaves and marble's faint glow light only a palm's width at night.

30165.event: You entered a backward race, tripped but slid to win a crown reversing your speech for a month.
30165.postEvent: Although communication was difficult, your classmates found it amusing, and you were even invited to perform in a backwards-speech play.

30166.event: You received an unsigned quill that turns words into butterflies at midnight; your relative thinks you mastered illusion magic.
30166.postEvent: The butterflies turn back into fragmented letters on the ground at sunrise, but no one can read them.

30167.event: Trapped in a fairy tale loop, you escaped by roaring like a dragon, scaring the butler unconscious.
30167.postEvent: After that, any fairy tale's beginning gave you a headache, and the butler still believes the dragon roar was real.

30168.event: A snail's trail forms tiny runes. You trace them and sparks fly. For three months, snapping fingers ignites hair.
30168.postEvent: Your hairstyle became quite avant-garde until a passing mage taught you how to extinguish the sparks.

30169.event: You entered a children's impossible tournament, made a stone hum, and earned a medal to hear wind names.
30169.postEvent: The names of long-dead people gave you insomnia, so you stored the medal in a box.

30170.event: You find a dusty mirror in the attic showing your future self who warns "Don't eat that apple pie," but nothing happens when you do.
30170.postEvent: You later wondered if that warning altered a small fate or was just a mirror ghost's trick.

30171.event: During magic academy lunch, you accidentally turned bread into a singing toad that predicted exam questions operatically.
30171.postEvent: After the exam, the toad sang its final song and dissipated into golden dust.

30172.event: The old armor hums, reciting your great-grandmother's adventures, and insists on teaching lost swordsmanship under the full moon.
30172.postEvent: Your learning was clumsy, but you did gain some strength.

30173.event: Gnome sold fake Dragon Tears; you dreamed of being cabbage nibbled by rabbits and woke up missing hair.
30173.postEvent: Later, you learned it was concentrated dreamscape dew, and the gnome was a wanted dreamwalker.

30174.event: You saved a griffin from a hedge; it thanked you with dead mice weekly until it learned to hunt goats.

30175.event: Blank dragonhide book; midnight showed dead's last words. You read: don't turn to last page.
30175.postEvent: You never dared open it, but sensed the boundary between life and death.

30176.event: In a knight duel, your sword became a trout, making everyone laugh—a bard's prank for teasing him.

30177.event: Lost, you met a tree spirit; its leaf promised a breeze but triggered sneezing.

30178.event: After a failed potion, you understand cat language only on Tuesday afternoons. You overhear a cat plotting to overthrow humans.
30178.postEvent: You gradually got used to it and even started asking cat dealers for information.

30179.event: A potion gave your shadow life; it mimicked you late, twisted eerily, then dissolved in a week.

30180.event: A future letter from you warns against tomorrow's egg; it's laced with Dragonfire Pepper causing fire breath.
30180.postEvent: The letter self-immolates immediately after you read it, leaving only a scorch mark.

30181.event: You break an ancient dragon tooth, its soul circles you thrice, hums a lullaby, dissipates, slightly boosting fire resistance.

30182.event: You find a broken mirror; your reflection winks and predicts rain. When it rains, the mirror shatters.

30183.event: You narrowly win a poetry duel with a disguised fairy, earning never-spoiling cookies that taste like burnt parchment.

30184.event: After seven dreams of chasing a glowing rabbit, you find its hair and gain faster running speed.

30185.event: You bet your breath with a dwarf, win seven rounds, bottle sighs. Uncorking, wind steals your hat and worries.

30186.event: Stepping on the Cheese King in the basement, you dream real gold and make a fortune.
30186.postEvent: Later, that cheese is snatched by a stray cat and disappears without a trace.

30187.event: You sleepwalk, talk to moon in Draconic, scratch craters with broom, ending eclipse. Next day, teeth dazzle.

30188.event: Sword sings love song for dead princess. Points deducted; lady lord invites. Sword mute; you sing, ruin voice.
30188.postEvent: The lady lord gives you a non-singing sword and a box of throat lozenges.

30189.event: Shadow drinks mead, signs your name. You wash dishes three months. Then free drinks; shadow sneaks drinks.

30190.event: You swallow dragon scale, cough out, only say 'My scales itch.' Dragon attacks; shout; it scratches; escape; dubbed Dragonslayer.

30191.event: You buy a fake repellent that draws bugs, so the guild hires you as bait, earning bites and commission.

30192.event: Magical paint turned your hair white, but one silver strand remains, glowing at night.

30193.event: A skeleton tax collector demands 3,271 joy-taxes; you pay 11 coppers and a tooth, which becomes honey-flavored gold.

30194.event: Silver mirror shows reflection one second ahead; you cut chin shaving; sell to baron who predicts breakfast menus.

30195.event: Boot runs off to find socks, meets its twin at cobbler; they were same deer leather; feet stay comfortable.

30196.event: A wooden box with a beating dragon heart hatches a misty nightmare-dragon, then grants dream flight.

30197.event: Kick talking rock; it's a golem's last consciousness; take to sunset; turns to gravel; palms soften rock.

30198.event: The Curdled Cheese Disease kills you, turning your body into cheese for rats as consciousness fades with church bells.

30199.event: Born, a star-browed surveyor touched your brow, whispering stardust forged, fate beyond stars, silencing your first cry.
30199.postEvent: Since then, whenever the night sky is clear, you always babble and smile at the stars.

30200.event: From garden soil you dug a warm dragon tooth that spoke of dragons, then turned to ash.

30201.event: You stumbled into an ancient forest, were raised by tree spirits, and woke with eternal bluebells in your hair.
30201.postEvent: Ever since, your hair always carries the fresh scent of forest dawn.

30202.event: Giant eagle took your cradle to its nest; you spent a night there; rescued by hunters.
30202.postEvent: From then on, you fear no height, and even enjoy standing against the wind.

30203.event: Meteors at birth; witch calls you calamity star; curse-breaker: curse is a door, push it to become a god.

30204.event: Your laughter turned bathwater gold. Parents summoned a mage; you blew a bubble and slept.

30205.event: A moon-white unicorn licked your forehead, leaving a crescent mark; family saw a silver glow fade.
30205.postEvent: The mark remained faintly visible until you grew up, making you especially endearing to living creatures.

30206.event: Your halfling nanny told of dancing skeletons and singing gems; your laughter levitated forks.
30206.postEvent: Later attempts never worked again, but your nanny firmly believed you had the makings of a trickster master.

30207.event: You ate a dragon-blood coin. You coughed embers for three days, then became heat-resistant.

30208.event: A dwarf carved a rune for giant strength. Instead, you made rainbow stinky farts for three days.

30209.event: You activated a teleportation array, appeared in a henhouse, and your father retrieved you.

30210.event: A bone dragon passed by. You screamed. It turned its skull. You stopped crying and reached out.
30210.postEvent: From then on, you have felt an inexplicable sense of ease around undead creatures.

30211.event: After dream mushroom spores gave you seven visions of talking animals, you woke with ancient calm.

30212.event: You drank a purple potion, turned into a glowing frog, and a stork ate you.

30213.event: You find "Dragon Chef's Notes" at a flea market; making "Scorched Scale Tarts" sparks a dragonfire blast, granting fire sense.
30213.postEvent: Neighbors complained about the smell of burnt scales, but a mage confirmed you have fire affinity.

30214.event: For three nights, your shadow fought the armorsmith's shadow, luring stray cats; it was a witch's prank.
30214.postEvent: Forced to buy the balm, your shadow became much calmer, but the armorsmith still thinks you provoked him.

30215.event: An ancient coin with weeping sun and laughing moon brought misfortunes until you cast it into a wishing well.
30215.postEvent: The wish came true, but the well now only spits out these coins.

30216.event: A monocled ghost lawyer harassed you into signing a soul disclaimer, then paid you a grievance fee for recruitment.
30216.postEvent: From then on, you take detours around ghost salesmen, but your wallet is a bit fatter.

30217.event: Your cloak billowed; villagers mistook you for a hero. You guarded a week; the real hero came; you fled.
30217.postEvent: Embarrassing as it was, the honey wine and roast meat that week were really good.

30218.event: Met a Chronos fragment in a forest, traded first love memory for eternal hourglass dust. Bought estate, heart missing corner.
30218.postEvent: Occasionally you dream of a blurry figure but cannot recall the name, only a tearful, moved feeling.

30219.event: After a summoning course, got a ghost horse named Hoppy that hates heights and appears three minutes daily.
30219.postEvent: Riding it always invites ridicule, but somehow your legs have become more nimble.

30220.event: Blue elven fireflies nested in your hair, humming at night. They left after a week, leaving normal hair and invitations.
30220.postEvent: In the city, a legend of the 'Firefly Lord' emerged, and you became a topic in some salons.

30221.event: Won 'Most Dull Adventure' as judges fell asleep during your tale of a three-day rat dispute. Prize: an itchy crown.
30221.postEvent: You suspect the hat is cursed, but it did make you more focused—because the itch left no room for distraction.

30222.event: The retired dragon baker taught you scones with dragon breath, scorching the guild hall; he paid gold.
30222.postEvent: You became the guild's head pastry chef, but your fire spells went awry.

30223.event: In Spiral Tower, sued by ghost for riding elevator counterclockwise. Fined 50 silver, recited code. Learned to keep right.
30223.postEvent: The fine pained you, but later you wrote satirical poems about this experience and gained some fame.

30224.event: A letter from your future grandson warned against eating fungi, but you did, summoning Myconid assassins and dying.
30224.postEvent: Your tombstone says you died from a mouthful of mushrooms. Myconid assassins curse your name on their altar for generations.

30225.event: You helped a talking cat claiming to be an elven prince; it was a runaway pet, and you got elven.
30225.postEvent: You became the only person in the city to be tricked by a royal cat, but the silver was real.

30226.event: You stumbled into an ogre-harpy matchmaking party, reluctantly judged couples, and got a slime pet.
30226.postEvent: The slime loves eating your old socks but helps clean armor.

30227.event: On your {age} birthday, you discovered that a doodle you casually drew in a tavern in your youth had been.

30228.event: While you dozed, an ancient magical surge turned your beard into star-stuff, scattering faint light.

30229.event: Your old scars glowed under the full moon, projecting a star map that attracted a cartographer, amusing and exasperating you.

30230.event: Your shadow mimics your old battle poses at dusk; neighbors whisper of war spirit possession.

30231.event: During a coughing fit, you expel a cursed seed that grows into a contradictory thorn bush.
30231.postEvent: You transplanted it to your backyard, and since then your afternoon tea has been accompanied by endless bickering.

30232.event: After three days searching for teeth, you found dragon breath embers that briefly restored your forties' strength.

30233.event: A Baku mistook you for a dream container, clearing your mind but erasing a childhood lullaby.

30234.event: Your hearing faded, yet you heard roots underground and an oak tapping for water.

30235.event: Rubbing an old gold coin melts it into memory light, showing a dungeon raid at twenty, but companions' faces are.
30235.postEvent: You stared at the light ball in a daze, uncertain whether it was a gift or a mockery of time.

30236.event: A dwarven spirit barrel cracked, spilling ancient runes that branded your hand, steadying your sword grip forever.

30237.event: After a mysterious fever, you see tiny numbers above each person's head—their remaining breaths, but not your own.
30237.postEvent: You avoided loved ones' numbers, but dreams woke you with endless counting.

30238.event: You discover a tiny clause hidden under ink in an old contract, entitling you to a free abandoned wizard tower at age 100.
30238.postEvent: You leaned on your cane to reclaim the property, only to find a nest of friendly ghost librarians living inside.

30239.event: One morning, your wrinkles began to squirm slightly like snakes, forming a line of ancient text: 'The seventh Dragon Frenzy.
30239.postEvent: You followed the instructions, but only saw a lost dragonfly collide with your sword, turning into a harmless spark.

30240.event: At a dinner celebrating your {age} birthday, you are choked by a magical grape from the Jade Tower ruins.

30241.event: Lost in a misty market after rain, you buy a ring; the alley vanishes, cold clings to your finger.
30241.postEvent: You sometimes feel the past's warmth and hear ancestral whispers when your fingers tremble.

30242.event: Under the rosebushes in the garden, you find a toad covered in golden warts.
30242.postEvent: When you place the rose before it, the toad transforms into an old mage with rosy cheeks and white hair.

30243.event: Horrified to see no shadow, the monk calls it an honor from light spirits; you search under the midday sun.
30243.postEvent: After three days, the shadow returns, willfully striking odd dance poses by candlelight.

30244.event: A dead friend's sealed letter invites you to Dusk Manor, where ruins light up and the dead toast you.
30244.postEvent: You drink heartily with dead comrades; dawn finds you leaning on a tombstone, clutching an immortal medal.

30245.event: In the attic, you unearth a yellowed parchment scrawled in dragon's blood ink with 'Ancient Dragon Pudding Recipe.
30245.postEvent: Afterward, your desserts always carry a faint burnt-dragon-breath flavor, but those who eat them feel a brief surge of power.

30246.event: Your left knee aches before rain until a sage finds a water shard predicting weather, so you carry an umbrella.
30246.postEvent: But occasionally, your knee aches for your fate, feeling like the eve of a storm.

30247.event: On a park bench, a stranger in a star-sand hood invites you to a game of chess.
30247.postEvent: The game ends in a draw; the traveler dissolves into ticking clocks. You forget last Tuesday but recall every move.

30248.event: For seven days a raven crows; you name it Night Ink; its feather lets letters arrive before sent.
30248.postEvent: Sometimes Night Ink flies back, bringing you shiny little trinkets stolen from somewhere unknown.

30249.event: Your reflection, a younger you, periodically teaches lost languages and sword techniques.
30249.postEvent: However, the reflection also mocks your wrinkles, and your bickering becomes a daily pastime.

30250.event: In a tavern, a drunken adventurer shows two fluorescent seeds, claiming they are 'Life Tree seeds' and that swallowing one.
30250.postEvent: Eating its sour berries lets you understand animals' gossip, mostly meal complaints.

30251.event: An invitation card with obsidian veins appears on your bedside, signed with a withered rose.
30251.postEvent: Upon drinking tea, pain fades, body becomes petals, mind finds eternal peace, yet story stays in portrait gallery for visitors.

30252.event: Buying an elf-ruins vial from a vendor, you uncork it, summon moonlight, dream of Elusa's kiss, and wake silver-eyed.
30252.postEvent: Then your night vision sharpens, but the full moon's distant song makes you weep.

30253.event: While gardening, a flower complains of drowning; druidic spirits awakened with age.
30253.postEvent: After adjusting watering, flowers thrive but gossip about neighbors daily.

30254.event: On an ancient battlefield, tossing a rune-carved die triggers an explosion, forming a tap-dancing skeleton that collapses.
30254.postEvent: Soon, you discover the die can influence low-probability events, like making a dropped coin stand upright or stopping hiccups precisely.

30255.event: You trade a worn coin for a parrot, once Eirashus's whisperer, now muttering draconic recipes.
30255.postEvent: Each full moon it recites dragon poetry; neighbors complain, but you feel the courtyard breeze warm.

30256.event: You find a rusted knight's emblem under a broken pillar, dream of a paladin's suicide, wake with a matching scar.
30256.postEvent: Under the full moon, the brand warms; painless, you recall borrowed combat skills.

30257.event: On a stormy night, a decrepit ghost ship drifts in the sea of clouds, and from it comes your deceased.
30257.postEvent: The next day, the old dog brings a cliff shell, humming your wedding song inside.

30258.event: You find a bronze door, enter an inverted starry sky, and a giant jellyfish asks you to write a letter to your younger self.
30258.postEvent: The moment the letter is finished, a few wrinkles lessen.

30259.event: After rain, you step on a mound in the forest.
30259.postEvent: Your argument turns tears to laughter; they name you Friend of Mycelium, gifting glowing mushrooms that make rainbow farts.

30260.event: After three days of turning anything you touched into salt pillars, your cellar brimmed with neighbors' gifts for free salt.
30260.postEvent: Your adventure earned you the nickname "Salt Hand" in town, but you refused a merchant's request to turn stones into salt.

30261.event: A gargoyle from an old church roof perched on your windowsill for seven nights, tapping the window with pebbles, insisting.
30261.postEvent: You talked for three days, it dropped an amulet saying "Better than epic poets," and your insomnia ended.

30262.event: At dusk in the cemetery, spirits tug your clothes, crying their shrouds are torn; you mend them with grass blades.
30262.postEvent: The next day, each repaired grave got a withered rose, and your clothes were never moth-eaten again.

30263.event: A frog knight leaped from your teacup whirlpool, said he'd waited 97 years, performed a sword dance, and vanished.
30263.postEvent: A small frog phantom appeared in your cup when bored, silently practicing swordplay, making tea taste better.

30264.event: Your shadow peeled away, bowed, went to the tavern, returned drunk with a female shadow, now strikes elegant poses.
30264.postEvent: Neighbors said your complexion improved, as if the youthful charm had returned.

30265.event: Swatting a squirrel with your cane sparked and turned its tail blue for a week—you found the Staff of Mockery.
30265.postEvent: You learned minor spells like self-stirring soup, useless in battle but fun for grandchildren.

30266.event: A storm of silver-blue butterflies swept through the town, each wing bearing the name of a deceased person.
30266.postEvent: After the butterfly flew away, you felt a lightness on your shoulder, as if half a lifetime's unspoken words had.

30267.event: You received an invitation written on a beech leaf, inviting you as 'the human who has witnessed the most sunrises'.
30267.postEvent: You alone can spot real magic items, but townsfolk think your taste is flawed.

30268.event: You exhaled colorful bubbles forming ancient scripts; in seven days you became bubbles, bursting into a rainbow stain.
30268.postEvent: Some say you were summoned as a jester by an ancient god, others that you became a rainbow.

30269.event: Spending a copper on 'How to Talk with Dragons,' you read a line; a distant mountain booms: 'What? Didn't hear.'

30270.event: Biting a Dragon Breath Chili at the market, you spit a flame; the crowd then awards you "Best Fire Breather".
30270.postEvent: For the following week, your hiccups carried a smell of sulfur.

30271.event: Your shadow springs free, crowns itself on the wall, bows to you, and returns by dawn as if nothing occurred.

30272.event: An owl with glasses gave you a postal license; you can hire night messengers, but they eat half your hazelnuts.

30273.event: Stepping on a golden leaf sent you to an elven feast; you ate seven dishes and returned with honey wine.
30273.postEvent: After that, you could no longer eat normal food, but each autumn equinox brought a mysterious golden leaf.

30274.event: A drunk swore he saw dancing skeletons. Later, a polite skeleton borrowed a coin and returned it with interest.

30275.event: A Dream Tax Collector charges emotional tax for heroic fantasies; you wake up missing three hairs.

30276.event: Your bright purple hair, immune to washing and cutting, reverts only after you retrieve an old woman's windblown hat.
30276.postEvent: A passing mage said this was a 'Little Curse of Dawn', specifically for curing morning grumpiness.

30277.event: You found a bronze ring letting you understand water. It complained about weather and fish, called you a bubble-brain.

30278.event: Goblins borrowed your cabin for three days, left it spotless and seven clay statues, one resembling you.

30279.event: You understood cats discussing upright humans, joined in, and all cats turned their backs on you.

30280.event: The Adventurer's Guild's bizarre quest for a lost imaginary pet gave you an inseparable friend.

30281.event: Deep in the forest, a tree told a lame joke, chilling your scalp. It laughed; you left it a stone.

30282.event: A knight blocked your path for a poetry duel; both forgot lines, laughed, and shared ale.

30283.event: Your timid shadow awakes on full moon nights, collecting fallen silver coins while you sleep, leaving them by your pillow.

30284.event: A future letter warned you not to eat tomorrow's blueberry pie; you did, regretted it, and got diarrhea.

30285.event: After rain, your glowing footprints from fluorescent mushroom spores attracted children for days.

30286.event: A stable horse said three snorts mean rain, a tail tap means fortune. You waited three days for the tap.
30286.postEvent: The next day, you indeed found three forgotten gold coins in an old coat.

30287.event: You were randomly selected to become a fluffy sheep for three hours, then craved grass.

30288.event: Forced by a Gypsy seer, your reading shows a fat duck; shocked, she dubs it "Duck of Fate" for absurd events.
30288.postEvent: Before she finished, you were carried into a haystack by a runaway pony.

30289.event: Your dagger claims to be an ancient hero's soul, but got its master's name wrong, despite peeling apples well.

30290.event: At an elven wedding, a bouquet hit you; custom forced dance with a minotaur, so you tap-danced all night.

30291.event: Chasing a hat into a well, you wished for a new one, but a water spirit returned your soaked hat.

30292.event: You bought a 'forever-full' pouch containing only a note on contentment; you kept it for its texture.

30293.event: After your name was forgotten overnight, three days of gesturing revealed a failed true-name concealment experiment.

30294.event: Dusting a temple statue, it awoke and rambled about wars. You couldn't flee until it finished a chapter.
30294.postEvent: As compensation, it engraved a defensive rune on your palm.

30295.event: Your perfect fried egg and inheritance of singing porcelain plates left you amused and perplexed.

30296.event: You freed a crow from a web; it daily brought you coins, buttons, a fake dragon scale, and its feather.

30297.event: In rainstorm, you saw a goldfish swimming through air, told all but believed none, yet later you could hold breath underwater ten minutes.

30298.event: Your bed vanished, found on the ceiling with a bed spirit's note. It returned next day, sheets neatly made.

30299.event: You drank a sneak potion, became ignored, took an apple from a store, then returned it.

30300.event: A mouse family used your lost gloves as bedding; seeing a baby mouse asleep, you bought another pair.

30301.event: You stumbled into a ghost tea party; polite undead taught you a spirit mantra and gave you an ice-cold, never-melting.

30302.event: A pink-and-white flower sprouted from your hair; a druid said it grants animal affinity after withering.
30302.postEvent: For the rest of the day, squirrels and sparrows circled you and helped retrieve a lost quill.

30303.event: A warning letter from the Time Administration Bureau deducted lifespan for daydreaming, but it was a child's prank.

30304.event: Your finger draws a glowing line, you sketch a door, open a tiny storage, fit a lunch, then the power vanishes.

30305.event: The mask revealed goblins and fairies, and wonder lingered after removing it.

30306.event: Walking on a stone road, your steps made a folk tune; passersby joined, dancing until dusk.

30307.event: A merchant sold fake dragon heart coal that glowed warmly through three winters.

30308.event: A wind led you to a valley where an apprentice dream traveler stole a childhood dream and gave you incense.

30309.event: In the Dragon's Nest Sanctuary, the dragon soul guardian's claws pierced your chest.

30310.event: Trained as a living curse container, you shattered absorbing an abyssal curse; your soul returned to void.

30311.event: You channeled the Withering Scream's curse, then exploded amid monks' chants, vanishing with the curses.

30312.event: The remnant soul of the dracolich attempted to fully seize his body during a lunar eclipse.

30313.event: She discovered a dark star, contracted star-rot, and after her final chart became stardust merging into it.

30314.event: Commissioned by the royal court to map the nation's star vein and earth vein alignments, she encountered an alien star.

30315.event: Based on the afterimage, she began secretly drawing the 'Gods' Star War Map,' discovering that the current constellation arrangement was.

30316.event: Dragon Scorched Wing melted fiefdom. Hero charged lair, suffered withered scar, died; his arm sealed flames.

30317.event: He traded his lifespan for dragon blood, then charged and died.

30318.event: He won a lich's phylactery with half his soul, but the curse consumed him; he dragged it into the abyss.

30319.event: He trapped a demigod, fused its divine spark, and became a war constellation.

30320.event: A rune experiment opened a dimensional rift, releasing an entropy demon that erased your existence, leaving only smoke.

30321.event: He sealed an entropy demon into his arm, turning to obsidian and becoming an eternal statue.

30322.event: She fell in love with a paladin; they sacrificed to the light, their souls merging into radiance.

30323.event: She used her lifespan to give her resurrected lover final moments, then turned to stardust.

30324.event: After eating purple-spotted cheese, you turned into cheese and were devoured by rats.

30325.event: A star-browed surveyor touched your wet brow and whispered "stardust-forged fate above stars," turning your cry into a starry gaze.
30325.postEvent: Afterwards, whenever the night sky was clear, you would always babble and smile at the stars.

30326.event: You got a blood letter from your future grandson, but ate a magic mushroom, leading to myconid assassins killing you.

30327.event: Your birth in a rose-and-gold castle was heralded by an ancient dragon prophecy.

30328.event: Reading knight legends in the family library, your clear pupils reflected a future of swords and magic.

30329.event: Rebels burned the castle at night; your father gave you a warm crest and fled through a secret passage.

30330.event: Adopted by an old mercenary, she learned survival on the streets.

30331.event: You enter the Adventurer's Guild, accept a cave beast quest, and vow to gain wealth and fame.

30332.event: Your bloodline awakens as a death knight runs you through; your legend ends, runes faintly glimmer.

30333.event: On the Night of Falling Stars, divine radiance pierced clouds, so you joined Temple Knights to learn Holy Light swordsmanship.

30334.event: You led your squad to breach the rebels' stronghold, facing your father's murderer with the crest, avenging him.

30335.event: Appointed a border count, you rebuilt your domain, but past traumas leave you alone beneath the cold moon.

30336.event: He led an army to save a half-elf girl from a dragon, but was grievously wounded.

30337.event: You flee with a half-elf girl carrying a deity fragment, faith strained by theocracy.

30338.event: He endured holy flames to shield a girl and turned to ashes.

30339.event: Born in a volcanic cave, your swaddle wrapped in lingering dragonfire, scales faintly glimmering in the candlelight.

30340.event: Found and raised by a mountain hunter, on every full moon night, the surging heat within him makes him uneasy.

30341.event: Demonic beasts attack village; he accidentally uses dragon breath, saves villagers, but they fear and exile him.

30342.event: At the magic academy, a dragon professor recognized his bloodline and took him as a disciple.

30343.event: During a dungeon trial, his dragon blood unlocks an ancient treasury, granting the Scorching Scale Staff and boosting power.

30344.event: During the Dragon Calamity, Dragon Slayers hunt dragon descendants, forcing him to flee in disguise.

30345.event: He befriended an ancient dragon, underwent a bloodline trial, and fell asleep awaiting awakening.

30346.event: Half-dragon transformation tears his flesh; after a year's sleep in the temple depths, dragon and human souls balance.

30347.event: Reorganizes the 'Dragon Scale Mercenary Group', using dragon breath as a banner, becoming the guardian of the border city-states.

30348.event: He challenged the Dragon Slayer leader, fell in battle, and his soul ascended in holy flame.

30349.event: Born in Eternal Dark City's Spider Mother sanctuary, she has silver hair, purple skin, ghostly blue eyes.

30350.event: Recalling her first surface raid ritual under moonlight, she yearned for the stars.

30351.event: Refusing to sacrifice surface elf, she was branded Traitor by Goddess Queen, exiled by mother on execution eve.

30352.event: She meets ranger master Starblade at the Underdark's edge, learning dual blades, stealth, and drinking moon dew to adapt.

30353.event: Hunts an Abyssal Spider alone, extracts its venom sac to strengthen her blades, and her reputation spreads among adventurers.

30354.event: Hunted by old clans after the Twilight Compact, she, a messenger, fell with peace letters strewn, unfulfilled peace perishing.

30355.event: She finds the ancient Moon Goddess's bow in ruins; its string summons star arrows, boosting her power.

30356.event: Her mother secretly pleads for help; she returns to the dark city, confronting her bloody-bladed sister, now Matron Mother.

30357.event: Having rescued her mother, her guilt-ridden soul loses speech; she vows at Starblade's grave to guard the border.

30358.event: During a rare rift, a demon army attacks; she leads rangers, charges, falls, buying time for survivors.

30359.event: Born in a graveyard hut, her cries drew crows, linking her fate to death.

30360.event: He woke a skeletal dog in the courtyard but played with it, showing an affinity for the undead.

30361.event: Entered the Imperial Magic Academy, expelled for forbidden books; a professor secretly gave him a necromancy manual.

30362.event: He stole corpses during the Black Death to test necromantic healing, saving his village but becoming wanted.

30363.event: He met lich Helma in ancient battlefield ruins, trading ten years of freedom for soul science lessons.

30364.event: After leaving the lich, he traveled alone, collected dragon bones, and created a bone dragon servant infused with dragon breath.

30365.event: The Adventurer's Guild ordered his extermination; he barely survived, shattered and dying in ruins.

30366.event: Performed the 'Phylactery Ritual', sealing his soul into a gem, becoming an undead lich while retaining his humanity.

30367.event: Built the Pale Tower in the undead plane, collecting masterless souls and researching the ultimate resurrection magic.

30368.event: As Ragnarök approached, he led his bone dragon legion to defend the world, using the power of death against chaos.

30369.event: Sealing the Chaos Gate, his phylactery shattered, his soul a guardian star over Eldlan.

30370.event: Born by the forge in the Glimmerstone Vein's depths, his father gave him a hammer-shaped jade pendant.

30371.event: First time holding a hammer, he forged an iron ring for his mother, revealing his talent.

30372.event: He saw his master forge a meteorite rune sword, causing an earthquake and inspiring his divine smith dream.

30373.event: At Gravel City's Hundred Hammer Festival, I beat thirteen veteran smiths, earning the title 'Son of the Anvil' and fame.

30374.event: During the Iron Vein Depletion crisis, he created an alloy formula that saved his family and city.

30375.event: For King's Shield, he infiltrated dragon lair, fought dragon, got bone but fatally burned, gave blueprint to apprentice, died.

30376.event: The king posthumously made him Royal Forgemaster; his daughter born posthumously that year, all honored his sacrifice, eternal glory to his family.

30377.event: During Dawn War, he oversaw forge, lost eye to molten metal, detonated furnace to protect it, dying with enemy.

30378.event: In the war's final year, he forged the Blade of Dawn from a meteorite, slicing the enemy general's enchanted armor.

30379.event: He retired after success, founded a Craftsman Academy in his hometown, and taught a hundred apprentices his skills.

30380.event: Aged blacksmith died peacefully by the hearth, smiling, still clutching his hammer.

30381.event: Born in the Jade Kingdom's duke mansion, celebrated, from a prominent family, prophesied to bring a golden age.

30382.event: Received top-notch swordsmanship and aristocratic education, exceptionally intelligent, and shone brilliantly in a performance before the royal court.

30383.event: Met the young prince at a palace banquet, the two developed a secret affection, and composed poems together.

30384.event: Rebels attacked the capital, killed her family; she escaped a secret passage but died alone from wounds.

30385.event: Joined a border mercenary group under a false name, honing survival instincts in a life of constant danger.

30386.event: Unexpectedly recovered the lost family emblem in an ancient ruin, uncovering the unfinished secrets of her predecessors.

30387.event: Poisoned by rebel remnants and saved by a wandering mage, he died from magic surge while awakening his potential.

30388.event: Under the guidance of a mage, awakens elemental powers in an ancient dragon ruin, becoming a magic swordsman.

30389.event: Returns to the capital, secretly contacts former subordinates, and plans a revenge scheme to overthrow the usurper.

30390.event: I slew my foe in the final battle, only to find a greater ancient god's revival plot endangering the kingdom.

30391.event: Inherits the family's legacy, is granted the title of Dame, and leads the allied forces against the darkness.

30392.event: He sacrificed himself in the ancient gods' war, burning his life for a glorious sword strike to defend the last gate.

30393.event: Born with a dragon's roar and scales, villagers saw him as a curse and nearly burned him.

30394.event: Lost control, dragon breath burned barn, expelled; but caravan abandoned him. Young struggling, old mercenary found him.

30395.event: Adopted by the old mercenary, learns to suppress anger and basic combat skills, gradually learning to get along with others.

30396.event: His dragon blood berserked on a dragon-slaying mission, causing both his foster father's sacrificial death and his own from injuries.

30397.event: In an ancient tomb, he finds a dragon stone tablet, learns his bloodline's truth, and vows to master his power.

30398.event: In a volcanic realm, meet a dragon elder, endure hellfire trial, learn dragon transformation.

30399.event: Becomes a legendary mercenary under the name 'Dragonfire', slaying countless ferocious beasts, gaining widespread fame.

30400.event: The kingdom warred with the dragon lord, but his traitorous bloodline made both sides hunt him.

30401.event: In desperation, he fully dragonized, burning the pursuers in fury, but gradually losing his human emotions.

30402.event: He established a sanctuary in the wasteland, sheltering the mixed-blood dragon descendants abandoned by the world, and regained his humanity.

30403.event: An ancient dragon attacked sanctuary. He became a blaze, scorched sky, died with dragon, soul to heaven.

30404.event: He was born a noble dark elf with silver hair, violet eyes, a Spider Queen mark, and an assassin's name.

30405.event: Entered the Spider Queen Academy, mastering assassination techniques and toxicology, excelling among peers, yet detesting the cruel sacrifices.

30406.event: Refuses to execute elven prisoner at initiation, sentenced to death, flees into deep tunnels before execution, disappears, fate unknown.

30407.event: Lost underground, sunlight burns nearly killed him, but old ranger saved. Dark elf body couldn't take surface light; he died.

30408.event: Followed the ranger to learn woodland survival and longbow techniques, cleansing the Spider Queen's curse with moonlight, embracing nature.

30409.event: The dying ranger entrusted her with Moon Shadow, ordering her to guard the Shadowy Woods.

30410.event: Encountered an adventurer and got involved in a dangerous quest to find the lost Elven Crown, pursued by underground kin.

30411.event: Confronting the pursuer leader, a former close friend, he shot a fatal arrow through tears, getting a crown clue.

30412.event: She recovered crown, refused throne, offered to Forest Council. Blessed by spirits, ascended as legendary archer.

30413.event: She killed the demon general with a shadow-breaking arrow, collapsed exhausted, and merged with the forest.

30414.event: Born into a scholarly family, as a baby he touched a floating codex and was deemed the chosen one.

30415.event: Displayed astonishing intelligence and was admitted to the Royal Academy of Magic as an exception, becoming the youngest apprentice.

30416.event: At the academy, he met a brilliant lover, and they studied life magic, sharing first love.

30417.event: His lover's terminal illness defeated all cures, and she died in his arms, shattering his world.

30418.event: He was expelled for stealing a necromantic text, then fell into dark, lonely research.

30419.event: The first successful summoning of the undead brought back his lover's fragmented soul shadow, but brought even deeper despair.

30420.event: Leave the Academy City, wander as a vagrant necromancer through graveyards and battlefields, collecting soul fragments.

30421.event: Fleeing the Church's Inquisition, you hide in an ancient tomb and fuse with a necromancer king's memory fragments.

30422.event: His attempt to resurrect his lover backfired, unleashing undead rampage, consuming his soul, and leaving him dead in the graveyard.

30423.event: Devour souls during a rampage, become a semi-lich, lose humanity, and rest eternally in an ancient tomb.

30424.event: Builds bone mound, becomes undead lord feared by living, but in midnight dreams still calls lover's name.

30425.event: To protect a kind village from a dragon cataclysm, he burns his soul, leads undead, and dies.

30426.event: Born at Ironforge's Core Furnace, his first cry awakens a runestone, an omen of a divine smith.

30427.event: Forge his first complete dagger in his father's workshop, during quenching it naturally manifests low-grade runes, drawing everyone's attention.

30428.event: Exploring an abandoned mine alone, he finds a meteorite and aims to forge a mythical artifact.

30429.event: Become a disciple of a reclusive rune master, train on the Anwei Plateau, forging elemental order into metal.

30430.event: He forges Star Breaker, severs a wyvern's wing, Ironforge cheers, and earns the title Rune Smith.

30431.event: The king commissions a royal sword, but the smith refuses a soul-devouring curse, is exiled, and his hammer rusts.

30432.event: Find the ancient dragonforge ruins in Far East Flame Mountain, channel dragonfire to relight the furnace and break mortal shackles.

30433.event: He forges Dragon King's Scale armor with dragonfire, dons it, slays a dragon, and becomes legend.

30434.event: During evil god's invasion, master armorer equips heroes with artifacts, turning tide, 'Anvil of the Mountain'.

30435.event: He forges Ragnarök to end the prophecy, casts his soul-fire, and dies completing it.

30436.event: She was born in the brocade swaddling of the Golden Leaf Princess, the castle tower bathed in dragon-flame-like sunset.

30437.event: Taught by a swordmaster and court poet, she danced with a silver sword and wove nets with words.

30438.event: During a coup, a rebel stabs a maiden; she falls beside her wet nurse, hearing father's 'Live on!'

30439.event: In a tattered cloak at the motley docks, she slits a thief's throat with a stolen dagger, tasting rusty freedom.

30440.event: She melted family crest into pieces, hung on Blood Oath Wings guild board. First quest: clear ghouls.

30441.event: In ancient tomb murals, she saw her enemy's emblem with the traitor bishop; tears blurred the stone.

30442.event: She pierced a lizard's eye with her family sword art, earning the hated nickname 'Bloodline of Silver Branch'.

30443.event: She cut a chain, leaped, nearly stabbed an enemy, but a holy knight's hammer fatally struck her. She vanished.

30444.event: Prison walls exploded. A one-armed knight threw a key, but she couldn't escape. She died holding Golden Leaf secrets.

30445.event: She raised the restored family banner high; three hundred mercenaries and seventy former troops charged towards the usurper's fortress.

30446.event: The enemy's head rolled before her father's portrait; she shed her shattered breastplate and vanished into border mountain passes.

30447.event: She carved the Silver Branch sword manual onto boards for street children, using her blade only for protection.

30448.event: She died peacefully on a snowy winter night; her ashes drifted through lands she once protected and lost.

30449.event: A baby's cry echoed among ancient dragon bones. A hunter found the scale-born child beneath dragon wings.

30450.event: Children threw stones, making him spit fire that burned the barn, so he was driven into the forest.

30451.event: The scarred dragon-blood taught the child to forge his anger-fueled bloodline into a weapon.

30452.event: He buried his mentor's dragon scale, picked up the great dragonbone sword, and set off for the Dragon Ridge Mountains.

30453.event: The ancient dragon asked the half-blood child to choose human or dragon, awakening dormant wings.

30454.event: He replied he wanted to see both ends, then traveled as Hunter of Scales hunting evil dragons and monsters.

30455.event: Dragon blood flooded him; scales covered half his body. His comrade's sword tip tremblingly pierced his chest.

30456.event: He lived alone on Misty Cliff, accepting lambs from villagers to dispel storms while studying ancient dragon magic.

30457.event: The holy knight's shield blocked his claws. That call pierced through the chaos: 'Remember the humanity you chose!

30458.event: He entered his birth tomb, used his last strength to become a coiled stone dragon, a mossy landmark.

30459.event: The Silverwing royal city celebrated a birthday; the bells rang through the skies. He was the kingdom's last hope.

30460.event: The coalition of neighboring kingdoms breached the royal capital; the king died in battle.

30461.event: An old knight taught him swordsmanship and honor on the frozen northern plains.

30462.event: The old knight died of illness. He joined the 'Gray Falcon Mercenary Company' alone, adopting the alias Erich, and first.

30463.event: In an ancient tomb, he learned his family guarded Silverwing, a holy sword that could sever causality.

30464.event: The mercenary company was framed by nobles and nearly annihilated.

30465.event: He rallied former soldiers and loyalists, establishing the 'Silverwing Restoration Army'. The royal bloodline rekindled hope.

30466.event: In the 'Battle of the Broken Throne', he held the holy sword and perished together with the usurper.

30467.event: Born in Dragonclaw Village with dark red scales, his mother died in childbirth; adopted by wandering sage Zaire.

30468.event: Zaire taught him literacy and spells to suppress the dragon blood's agitation; the cottage lamp warmed his gray childhood.

30469.event: His dragon blood first erupted in a nightmare, burning the house. Expelled, Zaire took him deep into the mountains.

30470.event: He found ancient dragon runes in a cave and intuitively mastered dragon roars through blood whispers.

30471.event: Zaire's blood stained his eyes red. The fire of revenge consumed his reason.

30472.event: Deep in the dragon tomb, he passed Wingburner's trial, gaining draconic might, memories, and shining scales.

30473.event: He joined the Adventurer's Guild as Redrock, teaming with an elven ranger and dwarven warrior, and gradually opened his heart.

30474.event: Amid the smoke of the Scale-Purging War, his identity was exposed.

30475.event: He led dragonborn tribes and allies in a counterattack at Burning Claw Fortress against dragon slayers, wings overshadowing the battlefield.

30476.event: Finally, he confronted the cult leader, unleashing a forbidden spell that incinerated both.

30477.event: In the Underdark's Shadow-Dance City, noble House Durla welcomed their star-eyed, milk-skinned eldest daughter.

30478.event: Forced to watch a sacrifice at Lolth's festival, doubt seeded her heart as starlight wavered.

30479.event: I fled the underground, was found and trained by wood-elf ranger Silverleaf, named Moondew, and my arrows pierced the air.

30480.event: Became the Whispering Forest's guardian, defending its border with twin blades and longbow, leaves rustling like a hymn.

30481.event: Pierced by a dark elf's poisoned blade, she slew the matriarch's heir with her last arrow, and forest vengeance fire consumed her and her bow.

30482.event: Fell for a knight of Lionheart Castle; moonlit vows, but their lifespans couldn't align.

30483.event: After her lover died, she vowed eternal chastity to the Moon Goddess, frosting her arrows with silver.

30484.event: With moonlight arrows she sealed the sentry, fell, and became light rain guarding border while silver leaves covered her eyes.

30485.event: Her flesh became stardust; her soul joined the goddess's hunt, lifted by silver leaves and deer.

30486.event: Ancient dragon god's heart pulsed in volcano; awakened mortal blood created Dragonborn shaking nations.

30487.event: In the final battle, they died to stop the Lich King. The undead tide halted; his name inscribed.

30488.event: Heart of Mountains roared; Balind forged runes into pistons, making golems and airships. Age of Power broke old shackles.

30489.event: She sealed the void rift with the Silver Moon, dying. Her longbow shattered; soul returned to moon's shadow.

30490.event: The night the Rose Emblem was erased, Edwin raised a rebel flag, gathering exiles and mercenaries for bloody scheming.

30491.event: With shattered star energy infused into mortals by arcane tower's burst, farmers wield starflame, turning magic from secret to wildfire.

30492.event: Ice crown shattered; dragon awoke. Its roar broke walls; city-states paid tribute. Scroll unrolled; lives became ruin or legend.

30493.event: War God's constellation faded; temple fires smoked. Prophet foretold Divine War: gods take avatars. Sky burns; soul becomes ash.

30494.event: Delving for treasure, an abyssal rift opened; grinning demons dragged you into eternal darkness, leaving only a faint ink trace.

30495.event: Your spear snapped in the arena; poisoned, you fell. The paladin's reward went to another.

30496.event: Withering Rose thorns burst; holy light died. You became a zombie. City gates shut; wailing drowned in darkness.

30497.event: Under the Arena of Fate's pillars, you fought outlaws and demigods, then fell as the artifact shattered and bells tolled.

30498.event: Answering the dragon-slaying decree, you marched north and were burned to ashes by Burning Heaven Wing.

30499.event: Arcane Tide caused magic chaos; mages lost control. Academy raised barrier; curfew and undercurrents.

30500.event: Dwarven Rune Gunpowder shattered knight dominance, revolutionizing mercenary markets and empowering commoners.

30501.event: Pale Whisper plague turned you into a walking corpse. Purifying fire destroyed you and undead; hymns faded.

30502.event: Dark elven ranger Saville raised a broken chain, igniting a slave revolt, war, and mercenary gold rush.

30503.event: Carland, the fallen noble turned Shadow Serpent, secretly spreads rebellion among commoners and tavern spies.

30504.event: Dragonblood awakened; people grew scales and dragon breath. Fear and worship spread; families prayed to escape.

30505.event: The Arcane Dome's floating city core overloaded and crashed, scattering ancient tomes and igniting a continent-wide treasure hunt.

30506.event: The gods' arena descends oracles from clouds; mortals pleasing them in bloody revelry earn unimaginable blessings, attracting believers and gamblers.

30507.event: Holy sword broken; knight order split, faith collapsed. War erupted; you died by a stray blade in a field.

30508.event: You exhausted mana in a bloody battle, pierced monster leader's heart with broken sword, died in cave.

30509.event: The Dwarven Bank bond bubble burst, the Blacksmith Guild collapsed, and society fell into chaos.

30510.event: Dragon Breath Ale at Crimson Volcano made you spit flame, singeing a dwarf's beard; real dragon saliva.
30510.postEvent: After that, every time you burped, a puff of smoke came out, lasting an entire season.

30511.event: Academy made you Honorary Bone Speaker for a crystal skull; a forgotten assignment revealed new death magic.

30512.event: Market rolling pin glowed, turned dough into chainmail. From smith Balinder, it now forges pastry into armor.
30512.postEvent: Your pies thus gained the reputation of 'edible armor'.

30513.event: Your right little finger crystallized into purple starstuff; dawn restored it, leaving star-like burns.

30514.event: Future descendant begs you to remove a dancing frog doll from your will to avoid a century-long copyright dispute.

30515.event: A gray owl lands on your shoulder with a brittle wax-sealed letter, signed 'Love, Leanna'—but you know no Leanna.
30515.postEvent: Every night you dreamed of a tower beneath a silver waterfall, a piano playing the same tune.

30516.event: On a stormy night, your reflection raised a glass; you copied, spilled wine, and it laughed silently.

30517.event: Guild seeks Softest Pillow to put red dragon to sleep. You recall down pillow from Dragonbone Ruins gave sweet dreams.
30517.postEvent: Turning it in, the guild paid you a retirement bounty and a shield inscribed 'Dragon Slayer (Sleep-Aid Category)'.

30518.event: At a dwarven funeral, you accidentally hammered a lively rhythm; the dwarves cheered, saying the deceased loved lively things.

30519.event: Dark elf ranger gave moonflower as past-life oath. You dreamed of mushroom forest patrol with twin blades, spider queen roaring.

30520.event: You could smell magic—burnt orange peel for fireballs, rain-soaked earth for healing—then it vanished after two months.

30521.event: While organizing the attic, you found a map leading to a well; its minty water slightly faded your wrinkles.

30522.event: Orchid learned Dwarvish, sang mining songs when you dozed. When covered, it sang mournful love songs. Neighbors thought secret lover.

30523.event: A rock elemental traded a gold nugget for socks later seen in a dragon's treasure hoard.

30524.event: At a dragon retirees' tea party, an elder's roar collapsed the pavilion; he blamed loose false teeth.

30525.event: Rusted cup offered three wishes, each needing 30 years chatter. You said 'peace and quiet', it fell silent 30 years.

30526.event: A bat delivered a tax letter; I tore it up and dreamed of losing a tooth.

30527.event: Figure used starlight quill to change wildflower colors, said destiny proofreader, a typo page 78. He vanished, cornflowers turned purple.

30528.event: Your white hair turned mood-changing sky blue, then faded to silver white after two months.

30529.event: You found a treant's tree-hollow restaurant; its Memory Soup reminded you of stepping on a rainbow beetle at age three.

30530.event: Griffin nested on roof; you removed its arrow; it gave you a feather that made you float.

30531.event: Invited to tell Lament War's truth, you spun wits into heroic rescue; awed youth crowdfunded wooden statue, its base bore "King of Liars".

30532.event: In an abandoned temple, a Mirror of Regret showed your regret: not buying elven cake. Your reflection licked it mockingly.
30532.postEvent: In a rage, you smashed the mirror, but after that, any cake you ate tasted of ashes.

30533.event: A distant uncle left a magical quill writing vulgar rhyming limericks; burning it only made raunchier songs.

30534.event: While relieving yourself in the deep woods, a glowing mushroom hummed an elven dirge that eased your fatigue.
30534.postEvent: Later, you tried to find it, but where the mushroom had been was only silence.

30535.event: A translucent owl delivered a blank wax-sealed letter; touching it showed yourself burying a coin thirty years later.
30535.postEvent: The next day, you dug and indeed found an ancient copper coin, but inscribed with your name.

30536.event: At Ironforge's Anvil Festival, hammer sparks became runes as an ancient dwarf's ghost taught you a secret technique.
30536.postEvent: After that, when you swung a hammer, you would occasionally strike out a small flame.

30537.event: You took shelter in a leaning tower; six mirrors showed nothing, but a central mirror showed you ten years older.
30537.postEvent: The man in the mirror nodded slightly at you, then shattered into starlight.

30538.event: After losing a bet to a gnome, you wore yellow trousers that sang about your rear for a week.
30538.postEvent: A week later, the trousers suddenly left you, reportedly bought at a high price by a narcissistic baron.

30539.event: You rescued a fairy; her pollen let you see sky gears and hear sneezes alter meteors.
30539.postEvent: After that, every sneeze gave you a strange sense of dread.

30540.event: At the market, a hooded figure peddles a 'Perpetual Poetry Pen' to you.
30540.postEvent: The pen eventually ignites in green flames and self-destructs, leaving a glass eyeball rolling out from the ashes.

30541.event: You judge a scarecrow contest, pick one in your gloves; it comes alive and reaps half a field.
30541.postEvent: The next day it returns to straw, but the corner of its mouth seems curved into a smile.

30542.event: A hermit teaches you the Yeast Eye Technique; bread fails but rosemary sprouts from your hair.
30542.postEvent: The hermit claps and laughs: 'You have attained the Dao—become a walking spice rack!'

30543.event: You push open an iron door inscribed with 'Silence is Golden' and enter a gray hall with towering bookshelves.
30543.postEvent: After you leave, the memory gradually fades, leaving only the inner peace you felt at the time.

30544.event: Your shadow plays Go with a withered tree's shadow; a torch forces it back, but you lose your hiccups.
30544.postEvent: Occasionally, when you feel stifled, you miss the shadow that played chess with you.

30545.event: You receive an anonymous package containing a backscratcher carved from a flame dragon's rib, with a note: 'When you scratch.
30545.postEvent: Thereafter, every time you scratch your back, you hear a distant dragon roar.

30546.event: At the Adventurers' Guild year-end banquet, you are mistaken for Darian, the 'Blackwing Dragonslayer,' and pushed onto the stage.
30546.postEvent: Darian hears of this and sends a dragon tooth talisman, noting the story outshines his deeds.

30547.event: While drinking by a stream, you pick up a warm, lard-like pebble with natural veins forming your name.
30547.postEvent: Three days later, that scene indeed plays out exactly, and you have salt ready in advance.

30548.event: Stepping on a glowing leaf teleports you into a sock; you meet a one-legged giant who gives dragon-bone coins.
30548.postEvent: You later sell that sock; apparently, items used by giants are highly sought after in collector circles.

30549.event: Your magical mimosa's caterwauling prompts complaints, but its lullaby for the captain's late wife makes everyone cry, dropping the case.
30549.postEvent: The mimosa now sings love songs, and suitors begin to appear under your window.

30550.event: A mustached knight pulls you into a cellar where the Outcast Chicken Brotherhood teaches weather divination with wishbones; you predict a fire rain.
30550.postEvent: The rain of fire was a mage's failed experiment, yet the chicken bone predicted it.

30551.event: You sneeze while praying to the forest god; he mishears. Next day, a piglet grows wings in your sty.
30551.postEvent: A circus buys the flying pig for a high price; you get gold but dare not sneeze while praying.

30552.event: A whirlwind lifts you up, and you land on the back of a giant whale above the sea of clouds.
30552.postEvent: After the moss withers, you often hear a distant whale song at midnight.

30553.event: A mage makes you burp soda on a flying carpet; it lifts then flips, dumping you in the moat.
