30553.postEvent: The old mage excitedly records the data, claiming that with improved recipes, you could leap over city walls.

30554.event: On a moonlit night, you see rats worship a holy cheese; the leader gives you an ancient coin, then they vanish.
30554.postEvent: That gold coin is later identified as coinage from a lost ratman dynasty, worth a fortune.

30555.event: Drinking dusty Frost Dwarf Fire Spirit makes beard grow wildly, then fall off, but you pay a hair cleaning fee.
30555.postEvent: Ever since, your neck itches at the smell of strong liquor.

30556.event: Passing by the arena, you are mistaken for an opponent by a squire, who shoves a broom into your hand.
30556.postEvent: The broom, named "Sweeping the Thousand Armies," was recorded and displayed in the guild hall.

30557.event: An ancient silver ring makes you write poetry uncontrollably; you remove it in fear.
30557.postEvent: You later give the ring to a bard, who becomes a best-selling poet overnight.

30558.event: You find a tattered Dragon God Cookbook, bake a biscuit, and attract a baby dragon that visits daily for treats.
30558.postEvent: You later learn this baby dragon descends from an ancient one, its saliva hardening into precious crystals.

30559.event: You dream of being guild chairman, then wake with a real badge your cat plays with, engraved 'Honorary Retired.'
30559.postEvent: This badge from the Adventurers' Guild gives adventurers over eighty a free tavern drink.

30560.event: While wiping the longsword you used in your youth, the blade suddenly hummed and a residual shadow flew out—it was.
30560.postEvent: Although you won the chess game, you found that you could seemingly sense the emotions of the chess pieces.

30561.event: Invited to teach dwarves a never-cold teapot, you mishear and forge a cussing one that swears in Dwarvish, a bestseller.
30561.postEvent: The dwarves gave you a patent fee.

30562.event: Your deceased partner invited you to his Styx tavern; you drank brew and felt ten years younger.
30562.postEvent: But thereafter, you would crow like a rooster precisely at noon for three days every month.

30563.event: You accidentally discovered a water elemental living in the ancient well in your backyard.
30563.postEvent: Later, the "Well God Wine" you brewed with it became somewhat famous in town.

30564.event: A dark elf girl gives you a talisman for resembling her grandfather; it lights night but turns your pupils cat-like in day.
30564.postEvent: As a result, the neighbor's children called you "Cat-Eye Grandpa."

30565.event: You revived withered roses with necromancy; they grew legs and tap danced nightly, keeping you awake.
30565.postEvent: In a rage, you tried to dispel the spell, only to find that they had become half-plant, half-undead beings, irreversible.

30566.event: While walking in the mountains, you rescued a griffin entangled in thorns.
30566.postEvent: That tail feather could summon a gentle breeze. You often used it to blow dandelions and amuse the younger generation.

30567.event: You stumbled into a lich's bookstore, forced to buy a book, then experienced three days of afterlives.
30567.postEvent: You grew indifferent to life and death, yet subconsciously chanted a funeral prayer before meals.

30568.event: You buy a "dragon egg" from a wanderer, hatch a gargoyle that brings dead mice, and its droppings become profitable fertilizer.
30568.postEvent: Later, you found that the gargoyle's droppings were top-grade fertilizer, and the pumpkins you grew were as big as carriages.

30569.event: Tuxedoed toad woke you, claimed to be Magic King, offered court mage job. You declined; got fly paste.
30569.postEvent: You later gave the fly paste to a poor alchemist, reportedly saving his life.

30570.event: A witch cursed you to become a pig, but you sneeze gold coins hitting the same neighbor every time.
30570.postEvent: You became a marvel in town, but every day you got a headache at the sound of metal clashing.

30571.event: Found a rusty chest under the oak with a note: "Still alive, huh?" It was a forgotten time capsule.
30571.postEvent: You laughed for a long time, then reburied a box full of jokes.

30572.event: You find your shadow disobeying: takes extra steps or stands up, and once waved at a beauty after your nap.
30572.postEvent: A Light priest said your shadow was just bored. You later learned to play checkers with it.

30573.event: Teleported to a floating city arena, you faked death and ambushed your way to second place, winning self-lacing boots.
30573.postEvent: Now you use those boots as flowerpots because the automatic lace-tying function always tied the laces into dead knots.

30574.event: A strange man took a minute of your time, whispered a decade-long forecast, and you became a prophet.
30574.postEvent: But in fact, you only knew when to take in the laundry.

30575.event: You lost a coin-counting contest to a toad at the Drunken Dragon Inn and croaked all day.
30575.postEvent: After that, whenever you heard frog croaks, you would instinctively cover your purse.

30576.event: A weeping white poplar in the Dark Forest held love letters from an elven knight, making you bitter.

30577.event: Three dwarves argued north-facing hammers bring wealth; you agreed, so they gave you a rune-engraved gold coin.

30578.event: A ghost crow dropped a letter with your name and date. You stood guard but felt a chill all day.

30579.event: Commission: Shimmering Crow took Merlin's dentures. You retrieved them from a spire. Got three Silver Moon coins.

30580.event: You stumbled into a skeleton waltz, danced stiffly, and left with flexible joints.

30581.event: Your shadow lags on twin moon nights; a witcher blames slacking shadow elves. Now it sizes you up.

30582.event: Balind's Rune Automatic Soup Pot exploded, leaving your hair spiky and blinding you for a week.

30583.event: Ancient ring from ruins made plants complain about drought, insects, pH. Headache made you throw it off.

30584.event: Vagrant claiming past-life brother gave you treasure map. You dug and found an old latrine.

30585.event: At a Dragon Breath BBQ Contest, you accidentally burned a barn, leaving you broke.

30586.event: Apprentice's botched spell swapped you with a cow. You convinced a farmer you weren't a cow demon.

30587.event: Your reflection at Silver Mirror Lake made faces, scaring you.

30588.event: The music box plays forever; at midnight its beautiful melody becomes a haunting hum, keeping you awake.

30589.event: Your market-bought bean grew into a sky vine overnight; climbing to a cloud cottage, you drank tea and all worries dissolved.

30590.event: Mistaken for an actor, you played a knight, shouted 'Dragon!', feigned death. Audience cheered; received a withered rose.

30591.event: Bathing, the soap spoke deeply, lamenting its fleeting fate: "Does cleanliness mean obliteration?" You pondered.

30592.event: Your cat brought a rune paper invitation. You traded three hairs for a never-out bulb; it never lit.

30593.event: A meteor crashed, revealing a dragon egg cookbook that goes blank when opened.

30594.event: Helped blind woman find dragon bone cane. She gave wish match; lit it, wish came true, match burned.

30595.event: You found your name on a missing persons list; a clerk crossed it out, muttering about restless ghosts.

30596.event: On a full moon night, you chased a firefly into glowing mushroom ring and danced with spirits until dawn.
30596.postEvent: Villagers named it Laugh Dance Fungi; secretly tasting a piece causes three days of laughter.

30597.event: Old woman gave you a star egg prophesying dragon wings. You incubated it; hatched a pebble.
30597.postEvent: You still wear that stone egg, hoping it will crack and roar like a dragon.

30598.event: Skeleton fishing with its rib gave you a spine promising water tribe loyalty; later you only summoned angry toad.
30598.postEvent: But since then, your fishing luck has been strangely good, always bringing back stew for dinner.

30599.event: A giant eagle dropped you in a haystack after sneezing. You avoided the sky for three days.
30599.postEvent: After that, castle towers and cliffs felt like flat ground, and you lost your fear of heights.

30600.event: You buy a dragon language book at a flea market; opening it dyes your hair a rainbow.
30600.postEvent: Bubble's residual magic heightens illusion sensitivity, letting you see through any disguise.

30601.event: At a circus, a dwarf spits sparks; you worship him, then learn fire-breathing, burning eyebrows yet forcing smoke.
30601.postEvent: Your eyebrows regrow quickly, but coughing now sends sparks flying, scaring cats.

30602.event: Imitating knights fighting a dog, you fall into a dry well, find Dwarvish runes, and laugh.
30602.postEvent: Later you hear that Balind was the dwarf master who forged the first iron giant statue.

30603.event: After drinking rainbow-grease soup, you see dragons smashing the moon, but the village sees only clouds.
30603.postEvent: The illusion fades; you foolishly obsess over building a moon ship.

30604.event: A letter bound in spider silk reveals a prophecy, but your father later admits it was a joke.
30604.postEvent: You still carefully fold paper into a sword, tie it with cord, and wear daily.

30605.event: You don a dark red cloak from mother's trunk, turn transparent, steal a sausage, but the magic fades, revealing you.
30605.postEvent: Punished to clean stables for a month, you learn to step silently on hay.

30606.event: Helping Uncle Blacksmith, you drop the anvil; it shatters, revealing a crimson fire seed. Uncle kneels and shouts 'Ancestor!'
30606.postEvent: The fire seed enshrined in Ironforge Temple; touching it makes you sensitive to furnace heat.

30607.event: A blind poet teaches you half a song, then forgets it; humming it makes young birds land on your shoulder.
30607.postEvent: You become the village's exclusive wedding singer, even though you only know the first half.

30608.event: Plant silver seed; pumpkin grows face, cries. Neighbor takes for stew. Drinking it, you feel ghosts aren't scary.
30608.postEvent: That bowl of soup keeps you from getting sick for half a year, but your burps emit silver light.

30609.event: Dancing at Moon God Festival, you trip; your ribbon catches fire. Priest laughs and gives you a crescent badge.
30609.postEvent: From then on, during every Moon God festival, you always get the most honey-glazed moon laurels.

30610.event: A black book tells a horror story; you avoid it, then listen; the knight is a ragdoll.
30610.postEvent: Now you can calmly flip through all kinds of horror picture books and even mock the illustrations for lacking detail.

30611.event: You found a rainbow scale, traded it for candy; it was lizard skin but the sweetest.
30611.postEvent: You keep the candy wrapper under your pillow, and it is said you can still dream of sweetness.

30612.event: A black cat asked for three moon rats; you got a field mouse; it took the reward and vanished into night.
30612.postEvent: Stomping in anger, you half-understand cat meows and now always hear their gossip.

30613.event: Two mages arguing about spells turn into sheep; you steal their wool and flee.
30613.postEvent: That wool, as soft as clouds, is knitted into socks that never let your toes get cold in winter.

30614.event: Found black pumice stone allowing water walking. You broke it, sank; dark elf rescued, said stones get annoyed.
30614.postEvent: You learn to swim, and also learn not to show off on strange things.

30615.event: The lord opened his hall for children, heard you shout for a dragon egg per family, and tossed a gold coin.
30615.postEvent: You hide that gold coin in your pillow, planning to actually buy a dragon egg one day.

30616.event: A meteor crashes; you find a soul-wailing black crystal, throw it in the well, turning water black; priest purifies.
30616.postEvent: Afterward, you fall seriously ill, but once recovered, you can even doze off while hearing ghost stories.

30617.event: A fortune teller said you'd be ogre chieftain. You unmasked him as a wanted charlatan. The guild rewarded you.
30617.postEvent: You become the town's little hero, but to this day, you hate eating barbecue.

30618.event: A friend dares you to touch a gargoyle's claw; it yawns, messes your hair, grumbles, and resumes snoring.
30618.postEvent: Your only injury is dust shaken loose getting into your eyes, but from then on, you become the child king.

30619.event: Kicking a rusty cannonball found with your pal in ruins, you trigger a rainbow blast, becoming glowing dust on dandelion fields.
30619.postEvent: Chrome flowers hum your childhood rhyme; your name on foam stone warns mischievous children.

30620.event: On midsummer night, a griffin left a starlight feather in your cradle. Your cries caused whirlwinds.
30620.postEvent: Your parents enchanted an amulet with a tail feather, letting you summon a minor tailwind once as an adult.

30621.event: Stumbled into magical garden, mandrake screamed and burrowed. Dream taught you to command vines using flower language.
30621.postEvent: The next morning, an intricate vine crown lay on your bedside, still glistening with dew.

30622.event: At age three, you stroked a cracked crystal ball, releasing a singing phantom that gave you timeless warmth.
30622.postEvent: Thereafter, when souls of deceased elders visited you in dreams, they always carried the same warmth.

30623.event: Mother's dark elf lullaby made you dream of silver trees. Your violet eyes saw spirit threads; cat demon bristled.
30623.postEvent: After your eye color faded, you could still occasionally glimpse elven runes in the corners of your vision.

30624.event: You swallowed a cotton candy cloud from a wind spirit; your burps made rainbow bubbles.
30624.postEvent: Your family grew accustomed to your rainbow rain and used it for fun at your fourth birthday party.

30625.event: Your cat became a dwarf and gave you insight. Your fingertips spark gold, letting you see through basic illusions.
30625.postEvent: Later you learned the cat dwarf was a retired court mage who secretly protected you until age five.

30626.event: At an adventurer camp, a dwarf tossed you; you laughed. He gave you a heavy, glowing adamantine die.
30626.postEvent: That die later became your lucky charm, always rolling the number you needed at critical moments.

30627.event: Digging in mud, you find a talking stone that teaches a Dwarven curse; you repeat it, shocking an elven gardener.
30627.postEvent: You later learned the Rock-Heart King, an ancient Dwarven earth being, taught you Dwarven words occasionally.

30628.event: At a Christmas banquet, you pulled off a viscountess's wig, revealing elf ears. She now smiles at you.
30628.postEvent: Your mother made you recite etiquette books, but the viscountess secretly rewarded your bravery with elven honey candy.

30629.event: You followed a glowing moon moth to an oak; its amber eyes showed ancient memories and planted wisdom.
30629.postEvent: Whenever lost, lean on a great tree to feel the earth's pulse and receive silent guidance.

30630.event: Father taught you swordplay; you threw it into a fire. A fire elemental made a charcoal dragon.
30630.postEvent: By age six, you could already light candles with a snap of your fingers.

30631.event: After a feverish dream of riding a white stag, you woke with a silver antler birthmark, rarely sick and tougher.
30631.postEvent: The village herbalist examined it and said it was a mark bestowed by the forest spirit.

30632.event: Goblins stopped your carriage, swapped your baby bottle for candy. You spoke ancient Elvish for three days, needing a linguist.
30632.postEvent: The linguist was amazed you learned a lost Elvish dialect, but three days later you only remembered 'candy'.

30633.event: A golden fish leaped into your hand, becoming a scale. Dipping it brought the fish back, splashing you.
30633.postEvent: Years later, you realized it was a token given to mortals by a water spirit in fish form.

30634.event: The wizard next door died on a lunar eclipse, bequeathing you his gargoyle that comes alive nightly to banish nightmares and fetch toys.
30634.postEvent: It became your most loyal childhood guardian until it completely petrified, its magic exhausted, when you were twelve.

30635.event: At age {age}, an invisible gray-robed friend saved your family from poisoned water, earning their awe.
30635.postEvent: You knew that the shadow friend always whistled a sound only you could hear at the moment of greatest danger.

30636.event: A giant cave rat bit your left arm; the blue wound scarred and granted near-immunity to underground toxins.
30636.postEvent: The doctor said it was due to the mutated magical essence in the cave rat's body—a blessing in disguise.

30637.event: Knights found you crying at the autumn market, made a shield cradle, sang, and brought you home.
30637.postEvent: The war song's melody, etched in memory, became a secret rally signal in adulthood.

30638.event: A music box put everyone to sleep; notes became stardust in your palms. Your laughter scatters starlight.
30638.postEvent: Your favorite toy made everyone sleep for ten minutes when opened, so your mother hid it.

30639.event: At {age}, you understood crows predicting you'd step in a puddle in three days.
30639.postEvent: Three days later, you fell in a puddle, crows laughed, and you lost bird speech.

30640.event: Your shadow turned into a dragon and ran away. You chased it across town until it returned.
30640.postEvent: An adventurer explains the dragon shadow was caused by leaking energy, but children now call you Dragon Foot Boy.

30641.event: A flea market ring teleports you on each blink; you die crashing into a smithy's trough, damaging the ring.
30641.postEvent: Sealed by the Adventurer's Guild, the ring still causes sightings of a fleeting teenage figure at twilight.

30642.event: A drunken sailor whispers you must dance with shadows thrice; later you trip, but the rest is a mystery.
30642.postEvent: After that night, you can't help but glance at your own shadow when walking.

30643.event: A black unmarked book catches your eye; its eerie melody invades your mind, and you hum it everywhere, startling all.
30643.postEvent: You gradually notice that the tune seems to make candle flames flicker slightly, but no one can understand its meaning.

30644.event: An old woman mistakes you for her grandson, feeds you moldy cake; three days of diarrhea, then an iron stomach.
30644.postEvent: The old woman never appears again, leaving you only a herbal rope knot.

30645.event: On a full moon night, you follow a dead comrade's call to a singing mushroom in a starlit forest.
30645.postEvent: Eating the mushroom smooths your skin briefly, but the song nearly traps you in the illusion forever.

30646.event: You accidentally drink a Dwarf's spirit liquor from your youth, bursting pores and making your beard rainbow.
30646.postEvent: For a week, you're the tavern's living attraction as everyone buys you drinks to watch your beard change color.

30647.event: A violent sneeze ignites the dragon breath in your chest, burning your walking stick into a charcoal staff.
30647.postEvent: Now everyone in town pays you one silver coin to light their stoves, giving you a stable pension.

30648.event: While summoning an undead pet in the graveyard, you get a skeletal rat stealing a sock daily.
30648.postEvent: You catch and cage it; watching it spin frustrated without socks makes days interesting.

30649.event: Wiping your old bow, its string fires an arrow into an oak whose bark splits into a phoenix.
30649.postEvent: A traveling antique dealer claims this is a divine bow awakened, and buys the bow for two hundred gold coins.

30650.event: Living underground so long, your skin now glows ghostly blue, making you a walking streetlamp at night.
30650.postEvent: Villagers worship you as a Moonlight God's envoy, bringing mushrooms and milk wine daily, but you can no longer hide.

30651.event: While organizing your notes, the ink bottle suddenly explodes. The ink condenses into an octopus, swallowing half of the manuscript.
30651.postEvent: You cry bitterly and rewrite, but unexpectedly derive a covered star magic formula, advancing your magical power.

30652.event: In a dream, the anvil god forges red-hot alloy; awake, you find it by your pillow.
30652.postEvent: You forge a self-heating dagger, sell it to a desert caravan, and get a bag of gems.

30653.event: In old age, your dragon scales shed in patches, revealing iridescent new scales like rainbows.
30653.postEvent: A traveling circus pays you well as a 'living rainbow,' but you feel like a display animal.

30654.event: You learn that your mortal enemy has died peacefully in bed. The hand holding the blood-stained old letter slowly lowers.
30654.postEvent: No ecstasy of revenge, only empty relief; you burn the letter and watch the stars all night.

30655.event: While strolling in the graveyard, you step into a skeletal hand. The five fingers grip your ankle tightly.
30655.postEvent: You'll have to take it home and use it as a doorstop. It occasionally gives you a thumbs-up as you walk by, as if complimenting your boots.

30656.event: Sleepwalking, you carve mysterious runes on a stone slab, but wake recognizing none.
30656.postEvent: A fortune teller buys them as prophecy runes for twenty gold coins, predicting three days' weather.

30657.event: You find a gold coin tree in a dry well. Picking a coin makes you forget an old friend's name.
30657.postEvent: With pockets full, you forget your purpose and dazedly trade coins for hot soup.

30658.event: In old age, dragon blood boils; you sprout wings, take off, but your strength gives out.
30658.postEvent: You crash into a haystack, and children laugh at you all summer as you climb out covered in straw.

30659.event: After drinking Earthfire Hammer in a bet, you were drunk three days and could understand goats.
30659.postEvent: But only goats. From then on, an old goat often stands by your window, making comments about you.

30660.event: A heavy rainstorm shrinks your shadow cloak, making it tight and short. You simply tailor it into a knee-length skirt.
30660.postEvent: The skirt turns invisible in moonlight; a dark elf mistakes you for a designer, making you rich.

30661.event: You cast a cleaning spell, but it turns the garden weeds into tap-dancing radishes.
30661.postEvent: Radishes dance all night, drawing the town, then wither to ash by dawn, but the story spreads.

30662.event: While asleep, your dragon tail unconsciously ties itself into a dead knot. You spend all morning trying to untie it.
30662.postEvent: You cut a piece off, but a silver new tip grew back, like a fallen star.

30663.event: Your fifty-year-old falcon suddenly speaks, daily echoing your youthful silly love words.
30663.postEvent: Ashamed to death, a bard buys the falcon for a hundred gold, saying it sings epics to life.

30664.event: You read a forbidden chapter and the words form a skeleton spirit demanding poems for knowledge.
30664.postEvent: Reciting a line made a book spirit give you a soul fire spell, then it dissolved into your palm.

30665.event: While carving a backwards rune on a toy dragon, it breathes real fire, singeing half your beard.
30665.postEvent: But you discover the toy is a perfect portable fire starter. The Adventurer's Guild places a hundred orders.

30666.event: You meet a unicorn with a crooked horn. Kindly trying to straighten it, the unicorn kicks you hard.
30666.postEvent: After a half-month without sitting, no poisonous insects dare approach you.

30667.event: While digging, you open an ancient dwarf wine cellar, and millennium-old beer foam blasts you skyward.
30667.postEvent: You land on a cloud, drift with the wind half a day, descend, and say clouds are cotton candy.

30668.event: Your skeleton servants suddenly go on strike, holding bone signs demanding an eight-hour workday and lunch breaks.
30668.postEvent: You laugh and cry as you agree; they work better than alive and brew you afternoon tea.

30669.event: A vocal cobblestone complained about your weight and cursed your shoes to puncture for six months.
30669.postEvent: Afterward, your boots indeed often get holes, but you learn to sense the earth's pulse barefoot.

30670.event: In a tavern, a monacled toad predicted you'd step in three puddles and left lotus leaves.
30670.postEvent: Stepping in three puddles, lotus leaves in your pocket emit a strange fragrance, attracting a herbal merchant.

30671.event: Armored pigeons besiege a sparrow; you feed them and get a stone medal.
30671.postEvent: This stone later turns out to be an ancient dwarf gold coin, merely encased in a stone shell.

30672.event: You wake with peacock-blue hair that won't change for a week, making you a spectacle.
30672.postEvent: After your hair color returns, you gain a hint of insight into color magic.

30673.event: A foul smell makes your words turn into soap bubbles for an hour.
30673.postEvent: Forced to communicate with gestures, you befriend a deaf-mute ranger who later teaches you a few silent step techniques.

30674.event: A crow in a nightgown falls, claiming to be a lost nightmare, and threatens lame jokes.
30674.postEvent: Sheltering the crow, its jokes freeze your blankets but it leaves a protective feather.

30675.event: You find a crown in a well; wearing it makes everyone bow until sunset.
30675.postEvent: The next day, the crown shrinks into a ring, but your demeanor gains an undeniable aristocratic air.

30676.event: A goblin's perfume makes you smell like leather but attracts friendly beasts for a week.
30676.postEvent: Carrying this scent through wolf territory, even the wolf king touches noses with you.

30677.event: On a full moon night, you see your shadow sneak off to clumsily waltz with a frog's shadow.
30677.postEvent: Since then, your understanding of summoning and illusion schools has deepened.

30678.event: A pigeon delivers a cryptic note about a forgotten act, leaving you paranoid.
30678.postEvent: You burn the letter, but the ashes form a smiley face.

30679.event: You find a sneezing oak in deep forest showering colorful feathers, gathering them to sew a pillow for soaring dreams.
30679.postEvent: This pillow is later bought at a high price by an insomniac old mage.

30680.event: A homeless man's eternal egg never spoils. It glows purple Mondays, then hatches a snail that grows glowing moss.
30680.postEvent: The moss became a rare enchanting material, exchanging for a few Gold Radiance Coins.

30681.event: Lost in an unmapped alley, a shopkeeper gave you a flying dream; you woke able to hover.
30681.postEvent: You bumped your head practicing hovering, but your levitation affinity greatly increased.

30682.event: Discovering a grinning copper coin, you pay and the baker laughs, giving double food; the coin spreads laughter whenever traded.
30682.postEvent: You used it to buy everyone a drink at the tavern, and the laughter that night shattered three glasses.

30683.event: You understood ants discussing honey bread and enjoyed free desserts for a week.
30683.postEvent: After losing the ability, you still looked for ants and learned to find edible insect nests.

30684.event: Sheep fell from bursting clouds, bleating, then grazing; people shed coats to collect their soft wool.
30684.postEvent: The Strange Weather Records noted that day; you knitted a forever-warm vest from collected wool.

30685.event: A ghostly letter invited you to a banquet with floating utensils; you could move the salt shaker with will.
30685.postEvent: Afterwards, you successfully practiced to telekinetically fetch books, though the distance was no more than an arm's length.

30686.event: Old witch's potion gave you wolf sense of smell; you smelled stone taste and fish stall for a week.
30686.postEvent: Your nose, now acutely sensitive to toxins after the medicine wore off, once saved you from a deadly mushroom meal.

30687.event: While sheltering in a cave, ancient war paintings came to life, telling you of glory and sorrow.
30687.postEvent: From then on, your ability to interpret ancient runes and inscriptions advanced by leaps and bounds.

30688.event: Your shoes went on strike over sweat-corroded laces until a cobbler silenced them.
30688.postEvent: Since then, you regularly sing to your items, and your dagger has become unusually obedient.

30689.event: A bird shadow tattoo made your shadow leave at dusk, returning with trinkets like petals and coins.
30689.postEvent: Once it brought back a real silver coin, paying for a day's meals.

30690.event: A music box's melody revealed dragon-riding memories and a familiar bond.
30690.postEvent: Later, when encountering dragon might, you remained calm, and a young dragon even judged you as a 'harmless acquaintance.'

30691.event: Abandoned on cathedral steps, a silver-armored saint found you sleeping peacefully under the morning star.

30692.event: Touching the holy sword Gift of Radiance, its blade hummed and a gold pattern appeared on your palm.

30693.event: Sworn in as a Templar knight before a crowd, Radiance Praise echoed through the cathedral.

30694.event: Investigating desecrated relics, you fought a beast horde alone, died covering retreat, and ascended to the gods.
30694.postEvent: The Temple held a grand funeral for you, and the bells of Radiance City tolled for three days.

30695.event: You learned the mastermind was an exiled paladin who secretly saved villages, cracking your faith.

30696.event: As the youngest Templar Grandmaster, I oversaw rebuilding the eastern fortress; banners flew again over Dragonback Mountains.

30697.event: You led Templars defending the Holy City seven days, then sealed the dragon with holy sword art, becoming spirit.

30698.event: The forbidden art's backlash left you near death; you passed the holy sword to a youth and peacefully ascended.

30699.event: Born in a stable to a wandering dancer mother, your first cry mixed with horse neighs.

30700.event: Eavesdropping on the old bard's song 'Dragonborn King', overnight you instinctively plucked the strings of a broken lute.

30701.event: You followed adventurers across Bone Wasteland, composing the epic 'Sparks of the Sand Sea' sung in Grey City tavern.

30702.event: Your song 'Tears of Elves' at Silvermoon's Midsummer Night festival moved the elven envoy, earning you a magical lyre.

30703.event: The Rose War erupted; you crossed battlefields writing knights' elegies, and 'Bloodstained Roses' shook the continent.

30704.event: Falsely accused as a spy, you wrote 'Stars Behind Bars' in prison, then rescued by an admirer.

30705.event: Hired by mercenary king Bloodaxe Regnar, you composed Northern War Songs and witnessed a brutal frost giant battle.

30706.event: Returning to your hometown inn, you found your mother's dancing dress and a dragon scale, inspiring 'Dragon Dance'.

30707.event: On your final tour, you performed at the Ancient Dragon Throne ruins as meteors fell, vanishing with music into light.

30708.event: Born beside an alchemy still, your father baptized you with dragon blood, and your blood has since shimmered.

30709.event: First success transmuting lead to silver revived the workshop, but cost your father a month of life.

30710.event: Admitted to the Royal Alchemists, factions ostracized you for unholy talent, but you found the fifth element's traces.

30711.event: A Philosopher's Stone fragment experiment in ancient ruins reduced you and half a district to ash, your name taboo.

30712.event: Exiled to Shadow Isles, you craft Storm Essence with a pirate alchemist, briefly controlling weather, and become an underground legend.

30713.event: You saved thousands with forbidden blood alchemy, but the church hunts you as the Blood Mage.

30714.event: You refine the Philosopher's Stone from a dragon's heart. Your body elementalizes; you journey to explore the world's composition.

30715.event: Final transformation at Astral Plane edge turns body to light and elements. Consciousness merges with laws, becoming eternal observer.

30716.event: Born during a lunar eclipse, your mother died; black runes on your arm marked an ancient curse.

30717.event: When the undead raided your village, you awakened your curse power, accidentally killing three guards—a lifelong nightmare.

30718.event: A wandering knight taught you swordsmanship and curse control, but your black flames burned him while you slept.

30719.event: The night knight died protecting you, his dying wish for purification at Northern Saint Sophia Monastery.

30720.event: In the monastery, you find the curse stems from a Valkyrie's sealed soul; only the Abyss Rift can sever it.

30721.event: The curse fully explodes in the final battle; your life flickers out, leaving only the Black Flame Knight legend.

30722.event: In the rift's depths, you duel a Valkyrie's soul, losing your arm to seal it and break the curse.

30723.event: Losing your right arm, you become Night Watch Knights instructor, teaching next generation to face auroral darkness.

30724.event: You return alone to rift, seal it with your crippled body, becoming a stone statue, sword pointing at darkness.

30725.event: Your mercenary father died in battle. You were born by campfire, first seeing a bloodstained sword and laughing courtesan.

30726.event: As a blacksmith's apprentice, you secretly trained like a mercenary, defeating three refugee youths with a wooden sword, catching One-Eye's.

30727.event: In your first battle, you carried a fallen banner at Grey Stone City, earning the nickname 'Little Hoof'.

30728.event: After the old captain's death, you were elected leader, reorganized the Iron Hoof Mercenary Company, and took your first big.

30729.event: Your thousand-strong mercenary band and you swear blood oath with the barbarian chief to fight ice giants; banners fly on ice fields.

30730.event: After Blood Canyon, you and the Twelve Iron Guards broke through, mortally wounded, collapsing in the wilderness.

30731.event: Through, Thieves', Guild, you, learn, Regent, of, Silvermoon, is, puppet, master, for, revenge, and, group's, name, you, take, assassination, job.

30732.event: You killed the regent, but One-Eye died saving you. Holding him, you swore no more brothers would die in vain.

30733.event: During the Ancient Dragon Cataclysm, you took the initiative to lead your company to aid the Holy City.

30734.event: A white-robed knight lifted him from the stone steps, washed away the dust with holy water, and named him 'Dawnlight'.

30735.event: A ray from his palm lit the ancient saint on the fresco during morning prayer.

30736.event: Through the dark Weeping Forest, he slayed the lurking ghouls, his sacred oath carved into bone and blood.

30737.event: Under a blood moon, he charged with the holy banner, turned to bone with his comrades, then silence.

30738.event: His most trusted mentor knelt to the evil god. The sacred relic 'Heart of Dawn' was stolen.

30739.event: In a secret chamber of a ruined monastery, he touched the true essence of the Holy Light—not faith, but sacrifice.

30740.event: Alone stormed the black dragon's lair, paid half a soul to reforge the shattered 'Dawnheart'.

30741.event: White-haired but with piercing eyes, sealed the relic into stone, waiting for the next dawn.

30742.event: Closed eyes before the stone throne, holy light bloomed for the last time, becoming an undying seed of radiance.

30743.event: In the circus caravan, you first touched your father's lute, clumsily plucking its strings.

30744.event: You met a silver-haired elf at Moonlight Lake, got the Wind-whisper Pearl, and your songs summoned the breeze.

30745.event: Broke into the hill where the ancient dragon slumbered, woke its soul with a lullaby, and received a dragon scale.

30746.event: Sang epics for the tyrant, secretly recorded his crimes, and at a banquet ignited the people's fury with poetry.

30747.event: The Black Knight burned the village; his dancer died shielding him, so the lute forever played a bleeding tune.

30748.event: A song from a lifetime of wandering summons phantom historical heroes when sung.

30749.event: Using heart's blood as ink on Pantheon's ruins, the final Covenant Song summoned an ancient dragon's soul.

30750.event: When the song ceased, he turned to stone, forever standing atop the ruins.

30751.event: Accidentally drank Liquid Gold Potion; his hair shimmered and all structures became eerily clear.

30752.event: Apprenticed to the Royal Alchemist, the first lesson is 'equivalent exchange' — trading left-eye sight for the 'Eye of Truth'.

30753.event: While refining 'Everburning Blood', an explosion destroyed half the academy; he was branded 'Forbidden One' and exiled.

30754.event: Protected by the Thieves' Guild, I researched banned arts and found the Philosopher's Stone needs living souls.

30755.event: To save the plague-ravaged Floating City, he secretly infused the Philosopher's Stone formula into the well water, turning all citizens.

30756.event: Set out seeking a Pure Soul, got Life Tree Sap in elven ruins, began reversing the curse.

30757.event: Using self as a crucible, dragon fire refines the soul, birthing the first perfect Philosopher's Stone: the Heart of Eldran.

30758.event: Born during a lunar eclipse, a black serpent mark made the midwife scream and toss him into thorns.

30759.event: The village withered at his touch; his tearful parents abandoned him in the wilderness, where he grew up among wolves.

30760.event: Adopted by Shadow Thieves, used cursed corrosive power for lockpicking and murder, became the Venomous Serpent.

30761.event: Fell in love with a temple maiden; her holiness temporarily suppressed the curse, but when the secret was exposed, she.

30762.event: Entered a desecrated temple, learned from murals the curse stemmed from the God-Slayer's bloodline.

30763.event: I underwent Three Gods' trial, slew inner 'sin' in illusion, and gained 'Dark Blessing' despite curse marks on left cheek.

30764.event: He directed the curse into the city-consuming void in the final battle, himself consumed by darkness, but the city survived.

30765.event: A scrawny youth killed a wild dog with a rusty longsword for bread during a famine.

30766.event: He joined Blood Wolf Mercenaries, got a wolf head brand, and traded his sword for drink.

30767.event: In an escort mission, single-handedly killed three griffin knights, was acclaimed as the new captain, and renamed the company 'Ironthorn'.

30768.event: Accepted a Lorraine title, defeated northern barbarians, but became a pawn in court intrigue.

30769.event: Falsely accused of treason by a minister, Ironthorn was massacred; he fled with survivors to the Valley of the Dead.

30770.event: Found an ancient dwarven forge in a canyon, reforged the Soul-Splitter Blade, earning the Dwarf King's respect.

30771.event: Led dwarven war host to capital, exposed conspiracy, became Marquis of Frontier, founded Shield of Freedom mercenary alliance.

30772.event: An ancient dragon awoke and destroyed the kingdom; he charged into the dragon's maw with the Soul-Splitter Blade, buying time.

30773.event: A foundling's cry at cathedral steps, a faded holy symbol in swaddling, foreshadowed an unbreakable divine bond.

30774.event: Chosen as a temple acolyte, you studied scripture and swordsmanship, learning the Holy Light defends truth.

30775.event: On your first Templar crusade, you met undead, raised the holy emblem in fear, scattered a dozen skeletons, and believed in Holy Light.

30776.event: Knighted as a Temple Knight, you vowed poverty, chastity, obedience, and were sent east to fight heretics.

30777.event: At Grantham, you broke the enemy flank with thirty horsemen, but the Grand Master's Blood Blessing clouded your faith.

30778.event: Secretly investigating church archives, you discovered that every generation of 'Saints' were refined as pillars to maintain the Holy Light.

30779.event: You were ordered to escort a holy relic, but freed a sacrificial maiden, becoming a hunted apostate.

30780.event: You meet heretical scholars who teach you to will the Holy Light, then recast your broken sword.

30781.event: The empire's Red Moon Plague struck; you saved over a hundred but succumbed, hailed as an uncrowned saint.

30782.event: The Inquisition orders you to repent or die; you ride to the Holy City with the awakened masses.

30783.event: Refusing to kneel in the Holy City, your death ignites a faith reform across the continent.

30784.event: Born in a traveling circus caravan to an elven dancer and human noble, abandoned under starlight, adopted by the ringmaster.

30785.event: You learn mandolin and juggling; an aged elven bard strokes your ear, saying you belong between forest and court.

30786.event: The circus passes through an ancient battlefield. Among the ruins, you find a broken shield inscribed with draconic musical notes.

30787.event: You leave the circus alone, becoming a wandering minstrel. In a tavern in the capital, you improvise a song exposing.

30788.event: In a library, you save a scroll fragment revealing the first king and Dragon God's pact was a betrayal.

30789.event: You sing at council, portraits cry. King orders tongue cut; dwarf saves with mechanical one. Voice changed.

30790.event: Dwarf saves you with mechanical tongue. Voice hoarse but magical. Flee north, compile Chronicle of Kings' Sins.

30791.event: Your forbidden poetry sparks uprisings in many cities. You're called the Flameback Poet with a huge bounty.

30792.event: Your song awakens an ice giant bird to breach the fortress. You learn song is the old gods' language.

30793.event: The king raids rebels. You shield with song, then fall. Your poem becomes a continent breeze.

30794.event: Born in Rust Town's mines, your father digs glowing ore. Your first cry turns it into a rainbow halo.

30795.event: Apprentice at a town alchemy shop, you trade mother's silver comb for a potion, losing its memories.

30796.event: You use talking ore to transmute lead to gold, but backfire turns you to golden statue; only ore glimmers.

30797.event: You steal a forbidden fragment. Learn the Philosopher's Stone is a god's heart breaking exchange law. Expelled, banished.

30798.event: Amid an Elemental Tide ruining old potions, you sell inferior ones from a border workshop, building reputation.

30799.event: You risk a transmutation to revive your sister, but she becomes an elemental, thanks you, and dissolves. You collapse.

30800.event: Find shard of god's heart. Touch it, see universe as exchange array. Truth overwhelms; you become light and merge.

30801.event: You attempt God's Alchemy, sacrificing memories, emotions, and soul for truth; your body turns translucent with arcane sparks.

30802.event: You touch the law's core, but your mortal form solidifies into a crystal lamp post at Truth's Hall.

30803.event: Born on gods' battlefield in black blood pool. Midwife turns to ash touching you. All birds vanish for miles.

30804.event: Hermit adopts you, binds arms, teaches meditation to suppress burning voice. You dream of black flames glassing world.

30805.event: Orcs raid; you unleash black flames, killing orcs and half village. Survivors call you 'Calamity Child.' Hermit takes you away.

30806.event: On his deathbed, the hermit unties your bandages: 'The curse is another side of a blessing.

30807.event: The witch-hunting knights track the black flame traces and surround you.

30808.event: You find records in ruins: your curse stems from a fallen god's spark, each reincarnation ending in self-combustion.

30809.event: A Blood Moon Rift spews old gods' servants; allies retreat as you hear the ancient god's call.

30810.event: You walk backwards into the rift, using yourself as bait, igniting the entire void with black flames.

30811.event: You are born in the broken Eagle's Nest Castle. Your father—the last baron—is stripped of his fief due to war.

30812.event: To repay debts, you shine shoes in taverns and tend horses in stables.

30813.event: You, rallying street orphans into Gray Eagle Mercenaries, led a goblin ambush and died from wounds days later in a tent under sunset flag.

30814.event: The kingdom erupts in the 'War of Succession.' The Gray Eagle Company is hired by a duke.

30815.event: Betrayed, you fight seven days as rearguard, then summon a giant eagle to escape, but wounded, you both fall.

30816.event: You redeem Eagle's Nest Castle, rebuild family. Company now five hundred; a brother warns you live by the sword.

30817.event: The new king distrusts mercenaries and disbands non-state forces. You comply, convert castle to orphanage, men return to farming.

30818.event: Invaded by 200,000, capital in peril. Veterans beg you to arm again; you don your old armor.

30819.event: Leading a hundred old soldiers at the bridge, you buy half a day. Exhausted, you die; enemy honors you.

30820.event: A paladin found a child in a border village, deemed his bones exceptional, and raised him in a monastery.

30821.event: As a squire, he prayed, practiced swordsmanship, read the Holy Scripture of Light, and excelled.

30822.event: Knelt in the Radiant Sanctuary after the Holy Flame Trial, knighted by the Archbishop, vowing to protect the people.

30823.event: Joined crusade east, wounded at Blood-Tear Pass, slew enemy deputy, awarded Shield of Saint, died smiling that night.

30824.event: Discovered the Heart of Light in an ancient shrine, was baptized, promoted to Knight-Commander, and now guards the Holy City.

30825.event: His closest friend fell to darkness, shattering his faith; they dueled in rain, and he tearfully beheaded his brother.

30826.event: Ancient dragon army besieged Holy City; he raised Heart of Light, charged, exploded into light rain with dragon's head.

30827.event: Born in a circus caravan, a harp and illusions by the cradle, blood infused with wanderlust and melody.

30828.event: Discovered by a silver-haired poet at the market, became his sole apprentice, and traveled to record heroic epics.

30829.event: Performed a play at a tavern, moved a poor noble, got treasure sponsorship and a recommendation letter.

30830.event: Alone traversed the Ever-Song Forest, lived with elves, learned ancient musical magic, and summoned healing light.

30831.event: At a royal banquet, exposed regent's conspiracy with song 'The Usurper', then assassinated and fled.

30832.event: Found the Lute of Sighs in a tomb; playing it spoke to the dead, but Church called him blasphemous.

30833.event: Poisoned by black magic, he lost his voice, retired in silence, and died with his harp, tears on his face.

30834.event: He lived alone in a remote lighthouse, wrote the Elderland Epic from his life, then died peacefully holding his harp.

30835.event: Born in a shack by the capital's sewer, his bankrupt alchemist father died in debt, leaving moldy manuscripts.

30836.event: Stole an alchemy book, got caught, mentioned an improved formula, and was recommended to the Magic Academy.

30837.event: Admitted specially to the Royal Magic Academy, a lab explosion burned his face, yet revealed the sulfur reaction path.

30838.event: Deciphered the Mercury Codex, brewed a Sage Potion, gained fame, and became an Academy researcher.

30839.event: Venturing for the Philosopher's Stone, a curse crystallized his arm; he then decoded alchemical secrets.

30840.event: Crystal emits final light; you become a crystal statue, soul trapped. Sage's Potion legend begins, but you're not dead.

30841.event: I found the Stone's formula needs a pure soul, refused the king, and was stripped of all, exiled to wasteland.

30842.event: In exile, you refine an Incomplete Philosopher's Stone from your crystallized flesh, extending life but losing humanity.

30843.event: The body disintegrates, consciousness becomes pure energy in the void, leaving a purple Philosopher's Stone.

30844.event: Born in Moon Crown castle, your crimson pupils prompt elders to seal your werewolf curse.

30845.event: Seal breaks; full moon turns you into a werewolf; you bite your brother and are chained forever.

30846.event: You escape a negligent guard, meet a one-eyed witch, and learn Moon Goddess's Tears cleanse the curse.

30847.event: Surrounded by undead while seeking Moon Tear in ancient ruins, I unleashed werewolf form near death and fought free.

30848.event: Join Adventurers' Guild, wear heavy armor and giant sword to hide identity. Only leader knows; teammates fear you.

30849.event: During the siege of Iron Wall City, you transform to save comrades, but become a hunted heretic.

30850.event: I found the Moon God's Tear, became human in half a year, loved and secretly married a kind healer.

30851.event: The curse returns stronger; during a red moon, you accidentally kill your wife, and your spirit collapses.

30852.event: Exiled to northern ice plains, you fight your inner beast for three days, then freeze to death smiling.

30853.event: Born in a blacksmith shed of Northland Iron Furnace Village, my first cry came with quenching hiss and great strength.

30854.event: Orcs raid your village, killing your parents. You grab a hammer, smash an orc, then join the Grey Wolf Mercenaries.

30855.event: You lead a squad at Blood and Tears Pass, kill over a hundred, and become the youngest decurion.

30856.event: After the captain falls in an ambush, you take his broken blade, swear revenge, and lead the remnants out.

30857.event: Knighted as Shield of the North, your mercenaries become a semi-official army at Icewind Fortress, reaching peak wealth and fame.

30858.event: The bribed vice-captain's sneak attack causes heavy casualties; you are captured and tortured.

30859.event: After prison, you gather men, use oil fires and traps to massacre traitors, then strangle the vice-captain, becoming iron-hearted.

30860.event: At Dragonbreath Plain's final battle, you baited the red dragon into a collapsed palace and perished together.

30861.event: The Templar Order declared a Holy War at the cathedral, summoning knights to crusade for gold and glory.

30862.event: Helmer's song awakens dragons; you yearn for legendary power.

30863.event: Hunted for the Philosopher's Stone, your magic berserk; you detonate in a ruined temple, turning to ash with enemies.

30864.event: A curse from the ruins turns your flesh to bronze, leaving you a statue in agony.

30865.event: Northern barbarians surge south. Adrian, Dragon Banner captain, rallies at Winterfell, promising gold and fiefs for brave blood.

30866.event: Ancient dragon Azeranos awoke from a volcano, burning half a province; the kingdom called the strongest adventurers to slay it.

30867.event: A forbidden experiment at Aivand Magic Academy tore reality, unleashing demons from a chaotic rift into bloody combat.

30868.event: The gods fall, sky shatters, fragments scatter. You touch one, divine fire consumes you to ash.

30869.event: The Adventurers' Guild's Golden Edict causes monster surges, doubled rewards, open ruins, and countless armed civilians to venture into the unknown.

30870.event: Necromancer's plague of undeath at the southern border turns you into a mindless undead.

30871.event: The king's decennial knighthood ceremony for border warriors sparked nationwide celebration.

30872.event: Sky dragons and humans forged a covenant: blood baptism made Dragon Knights, and youths flocked to nests for trials.

30873.event: The Templar Grand Crusade's call resounded, summoning the faithful east to reshape infidel lands.

30874.event: A wandering bard's epic of dragonfire spread across the land, igniting the flame of adventure in countless young hearts.

30875.event: The Alchemists' Guild mastered turning stone into gold, flooding the market and devaluing old currency overnight.

30876.event: Your shadow powers rage; temple hunters corner you. In a dark blaze, you die, soul consumed by ancient curse.

30877.event: The legendary mercenary captain fell in Ash Plains, his worn crown now a coveted token.

30878.event: The dragon, slumbering for millennia, roars, its wings eclipsing the sun; the kingdom bounties its slayer, but few return.

30879.event: The continent's top magic academy breaks class barriers, opening its tower doors to commoners, drawing a flood of poor students.

30880.event: The Black Death Mist seeps into your lungs. Despite the alchemists' efforts, you succumb to the plague and die.

30881.event: The king knights Paladins in a grand ceremony; cheering crowds, glory, and wine fill the streets.

30882.event: Omen of Ragnarök appears: blood rain falls, prayers fail, world falls into chaos.

30883.event: Strange noises from mazes; monster tides surge. Guild issues gold-tier bounty.

30884.event: The Bard Convention opens by the Xilona River; the winning poem is inscribed in the king's Mithril Hall.

30885.event: Entering tavern shatters wine glasses; mead utters Elvish words about stepping on invisible cat. Innkeeper denies owning it.
30885.postEvent: From then on, every time you drink, you hear a faint purring sound, but no one can explain it.

30886.event: Knight thinks your shadow has extra sword; begs you stand in sun to copy it for holy relic against dragons.
30886.postEvent: The Royal Alchemy Academy buys his sketches dearly, paying you a glowing copper coin.

30887.event: At the market, you buy a magical conch that predicts weather but hums the Paladin Anthem off-key nasally.
30887.postEvent: Every time it hums, it drizzles; on days you forget your umbrella, it's always sunny.

30888.event: A raven in tiny chainmail taps at night, handing a dragon-written parchment demanding the 20-year-old copper coin and a feather by sunrise.
30888.postEvent: You find the coin, place it on the windowsill, and a raven leaves a starlight feather.

30889.event: You learn snail language, but they only discuss salt horror and leaf betrayal.
30889.postEvent: When you report this to the Mage Tower, you are kicked out for suspected potion poisoning.

30890.event: Gnome Time Tinker stops you; says left sleeve three breaths shorter than right; insists on adjusting with silver hammer.
30890.postEvent: After three knocks, he leaves; afterwards, you always feel your left hand moves half a beat faster than your right.

30891.event: Your name inexplicably appears in the kingdom's Lost & Found as a persistent mist of lavender sorrow.
30891.postEvent: Claiming it bores mist into your eye, then reveals emotional symbols above heads.

30892.event: While peeing in the wild, you disturb a bear writing sonnets about bad honey.
30892.postEvent: It slaps you in shame and runs away, leaving a scar shaped like a musical staff on your left arm.

30893.event: A strange illness sweeps the town: all married people end their sentences with 'baa,' while unmarried ones say 'moo.'
30893.postEvent: Unmarried, you unconsciously say "baa," mistaken for married until a witch doctor explains your alpaca proximity.

30894.event: A yellowed letter from your future self warns: don't eat tomorrow's breakfast.
30894.postEvent: You hesitate then eat; it's delicious, nothing bad. Afterwards, you stop believing in time paradoxes.

30895.event: A ghost knight haunts your dreams, demanding a duel for using his sword bookmark.
30895.postEvent: You wake with a rusty sword, sleepwalking and shredding inn curtains into tassels.

30896.event: In an ancient tomb, you use a sealing scroll as toilet paper, freeing a djinn who tells bad jokes.
30896.postEvent: To repay you, it tells you three lame jokes daily, but each joke makes you sneeze violently with sparks.

30897.event: You kill a barrel mimic; it says its daughter is inside. Cut it open; find exquisite miniature tableware.
30897.postEvent: You sell the tableware profitably, but each full moon the pieces form a summoning circle.

30898.event: Help an old woman comb her starlight hair; she gives you a silver hair that mends dream holes.
30898.postEvent: You stitch two beautiful dreams together, then often have logically chaotic wonders in dreams.

30899.event: When counting money, your reflection secretly holds up an extra finger.
30899.postEvent: One day, it actually hands you an ancient gold coin from the mirror, engraved with your face.

30900.event: Sheltering in an abandoned church, you wished for angelic cooking; that night a six-winged angel fried you an egg.
30900.postEvent: The substitute chef claimed a wing cramp; after the egg, your back itched and grew two fluffs.

30901.event: You're dragged into a fairy court trial for shadow-harming a ladybug's self-esteem; twelve butterflies are the jury.
30901.postEvent: You apologized to the ladybug with a dance, earning praise poems on butterflies' wings and the elves' friendship.

30902.event: A goblin sold you a coin promising never upright; after ninety-nine flat flips, the hundredth spins forever.
30902.postEvent: The coin is now stuck in your ledger. Whenever you have a major decision to make, it hums softly.

30903.event: You found beetles rehearsing a play, and they made you a human statue in an oak-leaf costume.
30903.postEvent: After rehearsing all afternoon, you left, suddenly understanding insect whispers about bird droppings and morning dew.

30904.event: Your horse writes ancient Dwarvish on the ground with its hooves, complaining about stable food, signed with a hoof-print.
30904.postEvent: You took the complaint to the stable master. He was horrified and from then on gave your horse double apples.

30905.event: Bought Dragon's Sigh potion, broke it; blue mist made cockroaches perform epic scenes.
30905.postEvent: After your landlord fined you, you dreamed of flying and woke to a dragon scale.

30906.event: In a mirror world, death meant reviving then slowly getting hurt.
30906.postEvent: After climbing back to reality, you lost your sense of direction but gained the ability to walk on mirrors.

30907.event: You broke a prince's frog curse, but now he turns into a chair every three days.
30907.postEvent: Transformed into a conscious chair, he feared being sat on; reverting left his legs numb.

30908.event: You saved a drowning fairy, but she cried you ruined her Abyss Trial, forcing early retirement.
30908.postEvent: She gave you never-melting snowflakes that grant tiny wishes, like slowing tea cooling.

30909.event: At age one on a silver full moon, you sleep-murmured ancient dragon poems; exorcists fled, peace never returned.
30909.postEvent: The family sealed this matter and never spoke of that night again.

30910.event: Crawling in the temple garden, your tiny hand touches a withered rose that instantly blooms with golden dew; the gardener mutters ill omen.

30911.event: Found a glowing seashell; a mermaid's whisper taught you a haunting tide song.

30912.event: At three, you secretly wore your father's knight armor, got helmet stuck, giggled, then had a fever.

30913.event: You fed gray pigeons bread crumbs; they formed a crown cloud, and citizens called you Holy Pigeon's Child.

30914.event: Your cradle floated because the nanny's expired flying potion evaporated through the floorboards.
30914.postEvent: Years later, you always felt an inexplicable affinity for alchemy.

30915.event: At your first birthday ceremony, you ignored gold, holy symbols, and daggers, crawling to grab an ancient elven feather pen.
30915.postEvent: The banquet guests whispered among themselves: 'Another bard's soul.'

30916.event: After a frenzied goat knocked you over and gashed your head, you now fear all horned creatures, even unicorns.

30917.event: An old woman gave you amber candy at the market; you slept three days and woke to a withered leaf.
30917.postEvent: After examination, the healing priest claimed you had experienced a 'pseudo-death' and that your body was actually stronger.

30918.event: You built a crooked snowman that came alive at midnight, danced, then melted into singing water at dawn.

30919.event: At one year old, dying of fever, an ascetic's potion failed; you became glowing dust, leaving a silver hair.

30920.event: Splashing by the stream, a golden-scaled fish leaps and blows a crystal bubble—reflecting a burning tower and a silhouette—then bursts, and you wail.

30921.event: A green potion knocked over in a dusty basement turned into a palm-sized elemental, winked, and vanished through a vent.
30921.postEvent: Afterwards, you always dream of returning to that basement, watching the elemental sprite blink in the darkness.

30922.event: Chasing a cat at a Viscount's banquet, you knocked over a candelabra, igniting a skirt; family paid three months.

30923.event: Found a scaly egg, hatched a fire-breathing lizard; the Adventurers' Guild forced surrender, and you cried.
30923.postEvent: After that, you became more silent, but your strength inexplicably increased.

30924.event: Scratching your cradle wall, sacred painting fragment pierced your palm, leaving golden tattoo. The abbess said fate chose you.

30925.event: You found a rusty ring that turned your finger green, then vanished, leaving a faint mark.
30925.postEvent: After that, no one would ever shake that hand again.

30926.event: Hit by slingshot, you fainted and saw a six-winged golden angel. Priest called it concussion, but you believed.

30927.event: The sound echoes across the city that night during a lunar eclipse, and panicked citizens blame you for bringing divine punishment.
30927.postEvent: Your parents grounded you for a month, and the neighbors secretly called you 'Moon-Eclipse Child.'

30928.event: Dueling a scarecrow, you poked a bee nest. Face swelled like a dragon; you hid for two weeks.
30928.postEvent: Your mother applied crushed herbal paste to soothe the swelling, leaving a faint herbal scent.

30929.event: Playing hide-and-seek in a monastery library, a tome fell on you, and its runes taught you Mage Hand.
30929.postEvent: From then on, you became a menace in the library, and the nuns strictly forbade you from entering.

30930.event: At a funeral, you giggled, pointed at space, said uncle made faces. Mourners felt a chill.

30931.event: A Gypsy crone reads your palms, gasps, 'Left doom, right salvation—yet both empty?'—then flees, leaving gold.
30931.postEvent: The gold could support your family for a year, but the prophecy haunted you.

30932.event: On your fourth birthday, candle flames exploded, burning the house. You heard dragons chanting, saw golden light, and never woke.
30932.postEvent: A dragon scale found in ruins, clutched in your hand, engraved with a mortal-unknown name.

30933.event: You found a rusty copper ring; now you dream nightly of being a gargoyle atop Holy Light Cathedral.
30933.postEvent: A crow pecked your fingertip and dropped half a piece of parchment, with the words 'Return it' written crookedly.

30934.event: The widow's cat brings a blue-scaled lizard. It sneezes rainbows. She calls it dragon-kin; the cat uses it as a foot-warmer.
30934.postEvent: The lizard vanished; next day a dragon-scale coin with draconic breath lay under your pillow, worth ten candy bags.

30935.event: In abandoned mill, you found a dwarf roasting pebbles. He gave a hot stone, saying eat to understand wind.
30935.postEvent: Afterward, you could always hear faint murmurs in the wind, but you could never make out the words.

30936.event: While skipping stones, a pebble bounced seven times, exploded, and a frog appeared. It said you freed it.
30936.postEvent: After that, every time it rained, a row of neatly arranged dead insects appeared at your doorstep.

30937.event: A deer-masked vendor traded a singing shell for shoelaces, but its lullaby gave your family nightmares.
30937.postEvent: You threw the shell into the river, but the next day your shoelaces had retied themselves.

30938.event: You dug for candy, found dragon skeleton with your name; priest said to rebury and sprinkle holy water.
30938.postEvent: You did as he said. A month later, a glassy-fruit tree grew there, shimmering like gems in sunlight.

30939.event: A limping bard taught you half a song promising a drink, but its heretical lyrics got you thrown out.
30939.postEvent: A heretic scholar liked you and gave you forbidden pages of twisted truths.

30940.event: You hid in haystack, fell asleep, woke in stable, knight called you straw elf conscripted by warhorse.
30940.postEvent: He gave you a piece of sweet oatcake and said there is nascent holy light in your eyes.

30941.event: You found a wordless book; moonlight patterns seemed like sword moves, but three days revealed they were hay-forking actions.
30941.postEvent: Your hay-forking skills stunned the village, winning you the Harvest Festival pitchfork dance champion.

30942.event: A traveler in a tattered cloak drank water, turned into a raven, and left a healing wet feather.
30942.postEvent: The village apothecary bought this magical feather for ten silver coins.

30943.event: Time Merchant traded three-second rewind pearl for two hours of your life. You agreed; it restored a broken vase.
30943.postEvent: You started spacing out as if time was stolen, never recalling those two hours.

30944.event: Found tiny fairy cage promising sugar. Freed it; it grew, slapped you, called you idiot, and vanished.
30944.postEvent: Your left hand is allergic to sweets, causing rashes from sugar, so friends call it the sugar curse.

30945.event: Drunk blacksmith gave you a reverse dragon scale tattoo. You passed out; woke up smooth, but insects avoid you.
30945.postEvent: The blacksmith muttered that he once slayed a dragon when he was young, but this ink might be fake.

30946.event: You helped a witch comb her hair, got laughter honey, laughed all day, then felt great.
30946.postEvent: You blackmailed friends into doing homework by threatening laughing gas, but it was psychological warfare.

30947.event: You solved a talking door's riddle wrong, but it opened anyway, revealing a mummy and a note.
30947.postEvent: The mummy's shield bore your name reversed, chilling your neck each time you remembered.

30948.event: Sacrificial boy ran three laps; tree bloomed, dropped fruit with baby tooth. Elders said tree adopted you.
30948.postEvent: Each spring, a tender green sprout grew for a week where your baby tooth fell out.

30949.event: After secretly drinking your father's dwarf spirits, your fingers argued; you panicked and soaked them in ice water.
30949.postEvent: You found your middle finger is smarter—it always points to the hidden honey jar.

30950.event: A carrier pigeon brings a plea from your future self in a time rift toilet. Burning the note does nothing.
30950.postEvent: Three days later, finding the toilet paper empty, you suddenly understood.

30951.event: Fell into rabbit hole, upside down on ceiling. Rabbit family offered carrot pudding. Passed out, woke outside with golden carrot.
30951.postEvent: The golden carrot can be used as a flute. When played, wild rabbits will dance around you, even ignoring hunters.

30952.event: A knight left a clay pot of despair; you chipped it, releasing ghostly dog that licked your hand and vanished.
30952.postEvent: You cried all night, then stopped fearing the dark, gaining gentle courage.

30953.event: Bet friends on kick; shoe stuck on church cross. Priest called sign, made you clean. You found rusty medal.
30953.postEvent: Engraved on the medal: 'To a future saint.' You hid it in your secret box under the bed.

30954.event: A broken mirror shows your 80-year-old pirate self warning not to eat the neighbor's apple pie.
30954.postEvent: Terrified, next day neighbor poisoned by apple pie. You ran to thank him; mirror shattered, fragments spelled 'You're welcome.'

30955.event: A traveling merchant used your hair to weave a cloth showing you riding a pig in a storm.
30955.postEvent: A year later, you indeed escaped from an enraged turkey on a pig in a rainstorm, exactly matching the embroidery.

30956.event: Saved human-faced butterfly. It sprinkled powder; you dreamed you became one and joined others in crystal palace.
30956.postEvent: Waking with itchy shoulder blades, I grew two transparent scale wings that fell off days later, leaving faint rainbow traces.

30957.event: You buried a seed; the vine hummed an elven recipe that gave a mercenary his first victory.

30958.event: Mistakenly invited to a sage's centenary, a golden cake sang; a bite completed a chorus in your mouth.

30959.event: You directed a Templar knight to a grave. He returned with Morning Star sword and gave you an emblem.

30960.event: Bought an elven mirror reflecting your prime self. When weary, you feel a younger you travels within it.

30961.event: Your shadow refuses to rise. You promise adventure stories at night; it lazily returns to your feet.

30962.event: Obsidian music box in Sighing Corridor plays your heartbeat. Engraved: 'Listen, this is time.'

30963.event: Blind witch doctor: give coin to blue-haired child for youth. You did, felt agile, somersaulted, startling a knight.

30964.event: White deer grants three wishes; first two for fools. You wish for mead, a barrel soaks your robe.

30965.event: Invited to the Dusk Bride's wedding, your dead comrade and Maris toast you; the cup becomes rose petals, granting peace.

30966.event: Your cat speaks human, recites Dragon Calamity Chronicles at midnight. Druid says it's in awakening period.

30967.event: A merchant sells you a Can of Yesteryear; its scent makes you sneeze until dusk, scaring pigeons.

30968.event: A will gives you a Wandering Swamp that rises, spouts rainbow mist, and bows like a butler.

30969.event: You find a bottle warning not to look back. You do, but now hear footsteps every night.

30970.event: An elf wagers ten years of memories on chess. You lose, forget your stew, and eat bread.

30971.event: A youth returns a yellowed book you lent his grandfather; opening it releases an hour of dusk.

30972.event: You confess at Dawn Cathedral; your younger voice says you never followed rules. You fall and bruise your tailbone.

30973.event: Your name fades from the Book of Life; now you're the old man dancing giant in the square.

30974.event: A gargoyle invites you to a silent dance on a moonless night; you ache but feel younger.

30975.event: You buy a dream-recording quill, read your own adventure, find treasure, and wake with a rusty button.

30976.event: The compass points to an old woman who says her missing husband made it to find her in any world.

30977.event: You dream of being a World Tree. A golden leaf grows in your hair. Elven healer says it's harmless.

30978.event: A bard sings your life story as a female knight, then changes you to an androgynous twin elf.

30979.event: Mage sells dragon language for 10 gold; you only learn 'I am cheese.' Greet a dragon at your own risk.

30980.event: You find an old letter urging adventure, smile as sunset paints Eldland, then close your eyes forever.
30980.postEvent: Your name is engraved on the memorial wall of the Adventurers' Guild; legend lives on forever.

30981.event: You knock over holy water from an ancient battlefield; a hero's phantom calls you the Witness, leaving a burden.
30981.postEvent: From then on, every time you pray, you can feel the gaze of ancient heroes.

30982.event: Accidentally inhaling dried dragon dung as snuff, you sneeze sparks for three days and study dragon dung fireworks.

30983.event: A piano string snaps, cuts a chandelier chain; it falls, slicing a cake into sixteen perfect pieces.
30983.postEvent: You are thus invited to be the official "cake cutter" for major celebrations, and your income unexpectedly increases.

30984.event: You notice cursed lines match a meteor, but it's your grandson's ink prank. Amused and relieved.

30985.event: While gardening, you find a comrade's badge. That night, an old mercenary visits; you weep together.
30985.postEvent: It turns out he had been searching for you, the only one who might remember him.

30986.event: While cleaning a relic, you drop it in a chamber pot. It gains luster, declared a miracle. Rewarded but embarrassed.

30987.event: Attempting longevity ointment, you create super glue, stuck to a chair. You publish a paper on involuntary meditation.

30988.event: You chant from a forbidden book, summon a monocled toad librarian. Win chess, receive "Mud Divination."

30989.event: Your tears turn to gold from alchemical pollutants; the bride's family believes you bring wealth.

30990.event: At a tavern, you challenge a young man to arm-wrestle. Your shoulder dislocates; he's the champion in disguise. Consoled.
30990.postEvent: The young man leaves behind ointment and respect and departs.

30991.event: You revive a dead rose by chanting, but it's fertilizer water. Pilgrims swarm.

30992.event: You attempted to make the Philosopher's Stone from dentures, creating golden nut-crushing dentures, which you traded for ale and regretted.

30993.event: A cursed love letter combusts, smoke forms your lover's face. Arm petrifies, but you smile at the farewell.

30994.event: Carving a dragon rib harp turns cheerful tunes into dirges, making animals weep; tragic performances boost income.

30995.event: You escort a golden-egg goose; must feed it or eggs turn stone. It's a prank retirement gift.

30996.event: Your soul floats to clouds, plays Gwent with angels, loses, wakes with a holy relic card.
30996.postEvent: You become addicted to card games, always hoping to meet the angels for a rematch.

30997.event: While brewing, you mistake a phoenix feather for cinnamon, causing a prophecy mural about carrot snow that comes true.

30998.event: You dream the moon splits; waking, the cursed mark turns silver — a false alarm from wall powder.

30999.event: After ruining your voice, you ventriloquize dragon tongue, attracting three dragonets. Their mother gives a scale.

31000.event: You were struck by lightning while fetching laundry, unharmed, and your hair pointed the way for three days to a lost treasure.

31001.event: Fell asleep copying scriptures; muscle memory wrote a cat poem. You became a cat worshiper.
31001.postEvent: You framed the poem and hung it at your bedside, praying to cats every day.

31002.event: Wind blew your wig into a cauldron, forging a mind-reading hat. You gave it to your granddaughter.

31003.event: Your mourning curse turns flowers to ash, but family thanks you for ash sculptures making the funeral hauntingly beautiful.

31004.event: Your will demands your ashes in stained glass scenes of your life, a grand finale.

31005.event: Your silver beard became a rainbow; kids chased you as 'Rainbow Sage' for seven days.
31005.postEvent: A city painter created a portrait of you, titled 'The Final Rainbow,' hanging it in the adventurer guild's lounge.

31006.event: While sorting old things, a letter unfolds; a phantom comrade recites a forgotten vow to drink dragon's blood.
31006.postEvent: You went alone to the cellar, filled the long-empty cup, and drained it in one go.

31007.event: A gnome time tax collector took your life for a borrowed tea time.

31008.event: In a tavern, your dragon-slaying lie became real when the knight you invented thanked you.
31008.postEvent: You talked with him until dawn, and he dissipated in the morning light, leaving behind a faint dragon's roar.

31009.event: Stone slab collapses; you fall into a tiny dungeon. Chest has 'Surprise! Your lost youth' and an ancient coin.

31010.event: Your potted plant grows a fruit that tells Ragnarok dad jokes for seven days, then becomes a seed.
31010.postEvent: You buried the seed, looking forward to hearing new jokes next year.

31011.event: Adventurers mistake you for Magnus; you summon a thunderstorm to destroy giant rats in a mill.

31012.event: Bath water turns gold; skin glows for days. Elf calls it Twilight Blessing, making you radiant.

31013.event: A dragon fang splits open; a spirit recognizes you as master. It spits sparks and devours beef.
31013.postEvent: It perched on your shoulder, burping, and you smiled wryly, realizing your pension had another expense.

31014.event: Your shadow danced eerily under the full moon; your mirror reflection gained extra spirituality.
31014.postEvent: Since then, on every full moon, your shadow moves on its own, but you've grown used to it.

31015.event: Mistook dragon love letter as bounty. Climbed cliff, red dragon laughed, gave you a scale.
31015.postEvent: You don't understand the letter, but the warm dragon scale recalls that dragon's smile.

31016.event: An alchemy explosion turned your hair into living vines that bloom or bear berries, a druid's prank.
31016.postEvent: You learned to braid your hair with vines and occasionally harvest a few sweet-tart berries.

31017.event: A one-eyed crow prophet demands breadcrumbs but reveals ruins and lost spell fragments.
31017.postEvent: You gradually noticed that its reporting accuracy was inversely proportional to its boasting.

31018.event: Stumbling into a fairy ring, you danced with glowing sprites, ate honey candy, now run like the wind.
31018.postEvent: You still detest mushroom circles, but you have to admit that night's dance was unforgettable.

31019.event: You stole Cat's Eye Mead, turned into a cherished black cat, then reverted with a deadly elegant smile.
31019.postEvent: You swore to keep the secret, but the baroness is still searching for that 'well-behaved stray cat.'

31020.event: Sleepwalking, you join Temple Knights' training wrapped in a bedsheet. Mistaken for a monk, you wake stronger.
31020.postEvent: Afterwards, you deliberately 'somnambulate' to sneak in training until a Knight Commander cures your condition with holy light.

31021.event: To win ale, you disguise as a woman at a masquerade. Knights duel; a countess invites elope.
31021.postEvent: You earned the nickname Rose Knight, gaining deeper insight into gender.

31022.event: The rusty sword hums, guides your arm through a lost sword art, then shatters, leaving smoke in your hand.
31022.postEvent: Later, you seek sword masters, but none recognize the form; your hands still feel that day's sharpness when you execute.

31023.event: A blank parchment you took home as scrap paper was filled by morning with your entire day, including spilling milk at breakfast.
31023.postEvent: Following the diary's predictions, you dodge a fireplace collapse but miss a girl, then lock it away, afraid.

31024.event: While bathing, a magic circle appears at the bottom of the tub, sucking you into an underground river.
31024.postEvent: You now fear deep water slightly but know some merfolk phrases.

31025.event: A ring engraved "Do not wear" contains a dragon soul chattering about destruction and dog advice.
31025.postEvent: You gradually cannot tell if it is a mad dragon or a sage, but your skill in sophistry grows daily.

31026.event: Two archmages' polymorph duel turns you into a sheep; you bleat, eat, ponder, then recover unusually calm.
31026.postEvent: You become a vegetarian from then on and harbor a complex respect for mages.

31027.event: A one-armed mercenary pays with a counterfeit freedom coin; it's meteoric iron and gold, weakly resisting charm.
31027.postEvent: You wear it close all the time; though not valuable, it strengthens your will.

31028.event: You help a blind old woman find her 'lost cat,' only to find the cat atop the bell tower, gnawing.
31028.postEvent: Since then, you recover from injuries much faster.

31029.event: You join the 'Clumsy Knight Contest,' deliberately falling off your horse ten times in comical ways, making the crowd roar.
